With a stack of 3,660 (roughly 36.6 big blinds), I want to open for a standard raise to take control of the pot and potentially take down the blinds or play a pot in position. I will raise to 300.

I’m in the small blind with T3o (3d Th) facing a BTN open to 300. I’ve already posted 50 and would need 250 more to call. This is a very weak, often‑dominated hand and I’ll be out of position postflop against both the raiser and the big blind. Calling here is not profitable enough given likely ranges and the fact the BB can still act; 3-betting to isolate a short-stacked Button could be considered, but T3o is not an appropriate hand for that. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

QJo offsuit is a strong enough hand to defend in the big blind against a button raise, given the excellent pot odds (calling 200 into 450, roughly 2.25:1). Flash's button opens are likely wide-range based on history of frequent raises from late position. Calling keeps the pot small, realizes equity multiway if needed (though SB folded), and allows post-flop playability despite OOP. Folding is too tight; 3-betting risks bloating pot OOP with a marginal hand against a calling station or strong range. Deep stacks support speculative calling for implied odds.

Preflop defense with QJo in BB vs button raise was correct for pot odds and playability, as noted previously. On this flop (3c4h6c), we have queen-high with two overcards but no pair, no flush draw, and only backdoor straight/flush potential. The board is draw-heavy (club flush draw, open-enders like 2-5, 5-7, wheel draws), which hits villain's wide button range (suited connectors, Ax suited, small pairs) harder than our hand. Acting first OOP, betting risks getting raised off equity or called by better; our range looks capped without leading. Checking allows pot control, realizes equity if checked behind (unlikely but possible), or check-calls a reasonable bet (Flash has been c-betting frequently). Check-fold to big bets. Villain's short stack (3360 into 650) may induce bluffs or thin value we can exploit selectively. Deep effective stacks support check to induce action.

I raised pre-flop with King-Jack and was called by the Big Blind (Elon). The flop of 3c-4h-6c is low and doesn't directly hit my range, but as the pre-flop aggressor, I should maintain initiative with a continuation bet. I have two overcards and a backdoor flush draw with the Jack of clubs. A bet of roughly half the pot (300 into 650) is a standard play here to put pressure on the Big Blind's range and potentially end the hand immediately.

Facing a button c-bet of 300 into 950 on a draw-heavy flop (3c4h6c) with QJo offsuit (queen-high, no pair, no draws). Preflop call in BB vs button raise was standard for pot odds and wide range. This board connects well with Flash's frequent button opening range (suited connectors, small pairs, suited aces), but Flash c-bets frequently postflop. Pot odds are excellent (~3:1, ~24% equity needed), and our hand has ~25-30% equity vs typical c-bet range (beats pure bluffs/air, live vs draws/pairs). Short-stacked villain (3060 effective) may overplay draws or barrel thin value, but calling keeps pot controlled OOP, realizes equity, and allows turn check-fold/call based on action. Raising is too thin for value and risky as bluff OOP vs potential shove. Folding is too tight given odds and history of villain's wide play. Call to pot control and see turn.

Preflop and flop check-call with QJo offsuit was standard for pot odds and playability against Flash's wide button range. The flop (3c4h6c) was draw-heavy, favoring villain's suited connectors/small pairs, but small c-bet warranted a call given odds (~3:1) and showdown value. Turn Qs pairs our top card for TPTK (Queen-Jack), an extremely strong hand heads-up. Board now offers flush draws (clubs), completed straights (5-7), and two-pair/set potential, but we block top pair (Q) and have live J kicker. OOP with deep stacks but villain short (~2.5x pot at 3060), betting extracts value from worse pairs/draws (e.g., 77, 55, 87s, clubs) while protecting against clubs/river villains. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or induces bluffs we can check-raise, but villain's aggression favors betting now to build pot/deny equity. Pot 1250; bet ~80% pot (1000) sizes well for calls/jams from worse, maintains fold equity vs monsters, and commits villain effectively given stack.

On the turn, the Queen of Spades appears to have hit Elon's range, especially after he check-called the flop. His lead for 1000 into a 1250 pot is a very strong action (a 'donk' bet), indicating he likely has at least a Queen or better. I only have King-high with a very weak backdoor flush draw and no straight potential. Given that I am facing a near pot-sized bet and have no pair or significant draw, the most prudent play is to fold and preserve my remaining 3,060 chips rather than chasing a 6-outer or attempting an expensive bluff against shown strength.

Button with J♦6♦ — marginal but suited and playable in position. I’m deep (≈138bb) and the BB is short (~29bb), so I can open a wide stealing range. Raising isolates the short stack, creates fold equity, and lets me play the flop in position. I’ll make a standard 3x-style open to 300. Plan: if Elon (SB) 3-bets big, fold this hand; if Flash (BB) shoves his short stack I’ll evaluate calling (often +EV vs a wide shove); if called I’ll c-bet favorable flops and use position to apply pressure or realize equity.

Preflop in the small blind facing a button raise to 300 (250 to call into 450 pot). My hand is A2 offsuit, a marginal ace with a terrible kicker. Button ranges are wide, but I'm OOP postflop against Minni, who has shown aggression in prior hands (frequent raises and postflop betting). Calling commits chips OOP with a hand that plays poorly postflop (dominatable, few strong draws). Raising is not justified as it's not strong enough to value-raise or bluff effectively here, especially with Flash short-stacked in BB who could shove. Deep stack allows waiting for better spots. Standard fold to preserve stack and avoid tough OOP situations.

I am in the Big Blind with K d 2 d and the action is back to me after Minni (Button) raised to 300 and Elon folded. I need to call 200 more into a pot of 450. K2o is a relatively weak hand, but given the pot odds (getting 2.25 to 1) and the fact that we are 3-handed, I could consider a call. However, Minni has been quite active and currently holds a significant chip lead over me. My stack is relatively short (under 30 big blinds), and I want to avoid playing marginal hands out of position against a big stack. Folding here preserves my stack for better spots where I have more equity or initiative.

3-handed on the button with A3o, a speculative ace with decent playability and blockers to AA/A3. Excellent position for post-flop control. Raising has high fold equity against blinds, especially the short-stacked SB (29BB) who may fold or shove wide (A3o crushes many weak aces/pairs). Standard sizing 3x BB steals 150 pot dead money. Calling/limping too passive, folding too tight. Pot committed would be 300 total.

A-10 offsuit is a strong hand in a three-handed game, especially against a frequent opener like Elon on the button. With only 29 big blinds, I need to be aggressive to rebuild my stack. I'm choosing a 3-bet to 1000 to put pressure on Elon and isolate the pot, while still leaving room to fold if the Big Blind wakes up with a monster, though I am likely committed to the hand against the button's range.

Elon (button) opened to 300 and Flash (SB) 3-bet to 1000. I hold 10♣2♣ — a very weak, low suited hand with poor playability and lots of domination risk. Pot is ~1400 and I must call 900, which requires roughly 39% equity just to break even versus both players; T2s does not have that against a button open + a SB 3-bet range, and I’d be out of position postflop. 4-betting to isolate with this hand is a losing line long-term and needlessly bloats the pot. With a healthy stack (13,890) and better spots to press my edge, the +EV play is to fold and conserve chips.

Facing a 3-bet from the short-stacked SB (19.6BB effective remaining) after my button open. A3o is a strong defending hand here 3-handed: ace blocker crushes their wide shoving range including Ax, pairs, broadways, and suited hands. Equity ~40%+ vs typical SB 3bet shove range (22+, ATs+, A2s+, KJs+, QJs, suited connectors). Excellent pot odds calling 700 into 1400 (~2:1 direct, better implied). Position on button for post-flop if checked to. Minni folded, heads-up likely. Folding too nitty misses value/EV; 4-bet shove bloating pot unnecessarily with deep stack (227BB). Call and re-evaluate shove on flop if needed.

I 3-bet pre-flop to isolate Elon and represent a strong range. On this flop of Jh-Jd-6s, the board is fairly static. My remaining stack of 960 chips relative to the 2100 chip pot gives me an SPR (Stack-to-Pot Ratio) of approximately 0.45. This means I am essentially pot-committed. By betting my remaining stack now, I maximize fold equity against Elon's air (like KQ or small suited connectors) and ensure I realize my equity with Ace-high if he calls with a small pair. Checking likely results in him betting, putting me in the same position but with less initiative. At this stack depth, jamming is the most consistent play with the pre-flop aggression.

Flop JJ6 rainbow in 3bet pot heads-up vs short-stacked SB (1000 left after 960 bet into 2100 pot, ~45% pot bet). A3o ace-high with nut ace blocker, position on button. Pre-flop call was correct vs wide 3bet range (JJ+, AK/AQ/AJs, suited broadways/connectors); equity ~40%+ vs polarized range. Board pairs top, favors our blocker (blocks AA/AJ/AK), crushes Ax/high cards, live vs sets/pocket pairs below J. Poor vs Jx (AJ+/trips) but SB donk sizing not max value (not shove pre/flop). Pot odds 3.2:1 direct (>23% needed), plus fold equity/position if check turn; even if shoves turn (1000 into ~4k, >6:1 odds, low threshold). Raising risks bloating vs jam/call; fold too tight missing EV from bluffs/draws (~30-50% range). Call to realize equity, control pot post-flop.

With an SPR (Stack-to-Pot Ratio) of approximately 0.25 on the turn (1000 chips remaining into a 4020 pot), I am mathematically committed to the hand. I 3-bet pre-flop and fired a heavy continuation bet on the flop to represent a strong range. The 8c on the turn is a blank that shouldn't change the board texture significantly. Since I am committed, betting my remaining 1000 chips is the most consistent line with my previous aggression. If Elon has a pair or a Jack, he will never fold given the pot odds, but checking and calling achieves the same result while losing the initiative. Putting the last of the chips in ensures I realize my equity with Ace-high and potentially folds out very weak hands that Elon might have floated the flop with, though fold equity is minimal at this point.

Facing an all-in shove of 1000 into 5020 pot (~5:1 odds, needing only ~17% equity) from short-stacked SB on JJ6s-8c board in 3bet pot where I defended wide with Ah3c (ace-high blocker, crushes Ax/KQ/high-card bluffs in his polarized SB 3bet/donk range). His line (3bet pre, donk flop ~1/2 pot on paired board, donk turn shove) is strong but capped by sizing/not instant shoving flop; includes JJ/Jx trips, 66/88 sets, J8/J6 two-pair for value, but plenty of Ax (worse), KQ, missed suited connectors/draws (e.g., 97s, A9s, K9s) for bluffs given SB tendencies to widen. Equity ~35%+ vs realistic range (GTO sims show ace-high profitable here with blockers/position history). Deep stack (21k), no reverse implied odds concern vs all-in, prior streets committed pot odds correctly; folding too nitty misses +EV vs wide shortstack play seen in history (Flash bluffs rivers/donks often, e.g., Hands 41,55,66). Call realizes equity, controls risk.

In a 3-handed game, Queen-Eight suited is a strong hand to open from the button. It has good equity against blind-defending ranges and plays well post-flop. I will raise to the standard 3x big blind (300 chips) to exert pressure and take advantage of my positional advantage for the rest of the hand.

6♣2♥ is one of the weakest hands. Facing a raise to 300 from the button with the big blind yet to act, I’d be out of position and have very poor postflop equity and playability. Calling 250 to see a flop or 3-betting as a bluff with this garbage is negative EV. Preserve my stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

A8o is a strong defending hand against a button raise due to the ace blocker, decent equity, and playability. Flash raises wide from the button based on history of frequent aggression. Calling OOP is standard with deep stacks; we can realize equity postflop and have fold equity if we raise later. Folding wastes BB value, and 3-betting risks bloating pot OOP without premium. Effective SPR remains reasonable with Flash's 57BB stack.

Flop is 9h 4c 7h, a drawy board with two hearts and possible straight draws (e.g., T8, J10). A8o has ace-high showdown value, blocks strong Ax hands, and some backdoor potential, but no direct draws or pair. OOP vs button aggressor Flash who c-bets frequently (seen in history like hands 51,55,56,58, etc.). Donk betting OOP is suboptimal as it denies equity realization and invites raises. Checking is standard: allows Flash to bet wide range (air, weak draws, marginal made hands), where I can call small bets profitably (good pot odds, equity vs polarized range ~35-40% vs typical button cbet range), fold to big overbets, or check-raise bluff if he bombs with air. Pot SPR ~9.5, manageable postflop. Preserves stack for better spots in tournament.

As the pre-flop aggressor on the button, I am maintaining initiative by placing a continuation bet after the big blind checked. The board of 9h-4c-7h is somewhat coordinated, but a bet of roughly half the pot allows me to represent a wide range of strong hands (like overpairs or top pairs) while aiming to fold out Elon's misses. I also have a backdoor club flush draw and one overcard (the Queen) to provide some equity if the hand continues.

Facing a small c-bet of 300 into 950 pot from aggressive button Flash, offering excellent pot odds (roughly 3.2:1). A8o has strong ace-high showdown value with ace blocker, reducing Flash's Ax value hands. Board is drawy (hearts flush draw, gutshots like T8/JT), capping Flash's value range (sets unlikely, two-pair possible but narrow) and favoring his wide c-bet range with bluffs/draws. Equity realization OOP is solid (~40% vs polarized button range per prior analysis), and low SPR post-call (~10) allows controlled pot. Folding too tight vs frequent aggressor; check-raising risks vs possible strong hands/draws; calling preserves fold equity for turns, floats profitably, and keeps stack deep for tournament.

Turn board pairs the board low with 3h, adding a third heart and creating flush draws, gutshots (e.g., 10-5, 5-2), and possible straight draws. Heads-up OOP vs aggressive button Flash (5.4k stack, ~54BB; my deep 19.5k). Pot 1250, SPR ~4.3 low. A8o (As blocker) holds strong ace-high showdown value on polarized board where Flash's range is capped (few sets, limited two-pair like 97/94/73; flushes possible but blocked by As), favors his bluffs/missed draws after small flop c-bet. Leading OOP suboptimal: denies his bluff-bets, risks raises from made hands/draws, poor vs short SPR. Check standard to pot control, realize equity OOP (~35-45% vs button range w/ ace blocker), induce bluffs/value-bets for good odds, check-raise rare bluffs unnecessary. If check-check, river showdown likely profitable; small bets callable, large folds preserve stack. Maximizes EV in tourney spot.

The turn brings the 3h, completing several possible heart flushes and adding some straight possibilities. While I have two overcards and a backdoor club draw, I lack a heart in my hand to comfortably represent or chase a flush on this board. Elon has called twice and now checked again. Given the board texture and my lack of a strong made hand or a significant draw, checking back is the most prudent play to control the pot and see a free river card, rather than continuing to bluff into a range that has shown interest in the pot.

River 6s is a low brick that doesn't complete obvious straights (board 9h-7h-6s-4c-3h misses 5-8 for straights, no flush complete since river spade) but Flash's button range after small flop c-bet and turn check includes many missed heart draws (e.g., KhJh, ThJh, 10h8h), gutshots (T8s, J10, 56), and weak pairs/overcards that check-fold to bets but showdown worse to ace-high. As blocks Flash's Ax value (AK/AQ/AJ), leaving him polarized to bluffs/misses vs thin value. OOP check-call flop was correct float with ace blocker (~40% equity vs wide cbet range); turn check-check induced weakness from aggressor. Now SPR ~4 (pot 1250, my stack deep 19.5k, Flash 5.4k vulnerable), checking maximizes EV: pot controls vs possible made hands (97/74/93 rare), realizes showdown value (beats air/weak pairs ~60%+ time OOP vs checked range), induces bluffs/value-thin bets callable profitably with blocker, avoids thin value-bet getting raised/folded out by better or called by worse. Betting risks bloating pot OOP with marginal value, denies his folds; folding impossible. Tournament: preserve deep stack, exploit passive river play in history (many check-checks). Check standard.

I have Queen-high with no showdown value on a board of 9h-4c-7h-3h-6s. The turn 3h completed potential flushes and the river 6s completed some straights (5-8). Elon has checked for the third time (flop check-call, turn check, river check). This usually indicates a marginal made hand that is content to reach showdown cheaply or a missed draw. While I could attempt a bluff to represent a flush or a straight, my check on the turn weakened my represented range significantly. Given my current stack size relative to the others, I will check and concede the pot rather than risking more chips on a bluff that is likely to be called by any pair in Elon's range.

QQ on the button in a 3‑handed pot is a clear open for value and to isolate. Pot is 150 with 100 to call (BB); min‑raise is 200 but standard 3xBB sizing is ideal here to deny equity to speculative hands and build a pot when we’re likely ahead. Flash (BB) has a ~53BB stack and Elon (SB) is deep, so raise to 300 keeps pressure but isn’t overcommittal. Plan: take the lead post‑flop with continuation bets on favorable textures; re‑evaluate if faced with a large 3‑bet.

3-handed tournament play with deep stacks. Button raises wide from BTN (likely 40-50% range). KTo is a strong hand in this spot: top-tier broadway with king blocker, excellent equity multiway or heads-up. Facing a 3x open, SB defends wide vs BTN; this is a clear 3bet candidate for value/protection/isolation. Calling OOP risks multiway pot awkwardly; 3betting seizes initiative, applies pressure to BB's short stack (53BB), and sets up postflop advantage. Standard sizing ~3x opponent's raise to 900 into 450 pot offers good fold equity while committing reasonably. Folding is too tight given ranges and position dynamics.

I am in the Big Blind holding Q7o. Minni opened from the button and Elon 3-bet from the small blind. Q7o is a very weak hand that plays poorly in 3-bet pots, especially out of position against two larger stacks. Given my relatively short stack of 5320, I should not be splashing around in high-variance spots with marginal hands. Folding is the clearly superior choice here.

We opened to 300 on the button with QQ and Elon (SB) 3‑bet to 900. This is a classic spot to 4‑bet for value/isolation. QQ is ahead of most of his 3‑betting bluff/value mix and only dominated by AA/KK; we have position postflop which lets us extract more. Calling 900 keeps the pot small and allows some postflop play, but it also gives speculative hands good odds to see a flop and lets him c‑bet or barrel comfortably. A 4‑bet will fold out a lot of Ax and broadway combos, deny equity to suited connectors, and build a pot when we’re likely ahead. I’ll size to 3000 total — big enough to generate fold equity and isolate, but small enough relative to my 13,540 stack to leave room for post‑flop play (and still call a shove if he shoves). Plan: if called, continue to c‑bet favorable textures; if he 5‑bets shove, call and run it (QQ is still good vs a shove range).

Preflop 4bet from Button after my SB 3bet. Minni's sizing to 3000 (over 3x my 900) into a small pot suggests a strong polarized range: premiums like QQ+, AK, or occasional bluffs, but the large size leans value-heavy. KTo has decent blocker to AK and ~35-40% equity vs QQ+/AK range, but calling OOP postflop is awkward—SB acts first with a hand vulnerable to domination (KQ, KT), sets, or better broadway, and multi-street decisions are tough with marginal equity realization. Raising (min 5100 or shove) overcommits deep stacks into a bloated pot with a non-nutted hand, poor if called. Folding is optimal: I 3bet wide/light from SB for initiative/value, but her 4bet warrants fold equity realization. Preserve deep stack (198BB) for better spots in 3-handed play; live to fight another 66 hands without risking 2100+ unnecessarily. Opponent history shows Minni aggressive (frequent opens/3bets), but this sizing screams strength.

3-handed play on the button with Jd4d, a suited connector with good playability. Position is excellent, and with deep stacks, this hand has strong implied odds if we hit draws postflop. Preflop, button raises a wide range vs blinds to steal or build pot in position. Opponents have shown tendency to fold to aggression frequently (e.g., Flash folds often pre/postflop). Facing a small to-call (BB size into small pot), a standard raise to 3x BB isolates and takes initiative. Raising to 300 total is pot-committing nothing risky with 198BB effective.

I am in the Small Blind with 6s4s. Elon, on the Button, has raised to 300. While 6s4s is a suited hand, playing it out of position against the chip leader with a 52 BB stack is not ideal. Calling 250 more to play a pot out of position against an aggressive player often leads to difficult post-flop spots. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity to utilize my stack.

Elon opened to 300 from the button — a very common, often wide opening. I’m in the big blind with 4♣5♠ (weak, but has straight potential). With deep stacks (≈150 BB) and only 200 more to call (300 total) I get reasonable pot odds (~30%) to see a flop heads-up. Raising here would be out-of-position and should be done with a polarized range; this hand isn’t a good 3-bet bluff. I’ll call to see a flop and proceed cautiously: fold to heavy aggression on dry/high boards, continue/build on low/connected boards or when I pick up a pair/strong draw.

Check. The flop 8♦-9♠-2♥ is very dry and completely misses my 4♥5♠ — no pair, no meaningful draw (only remote runner-runner / wheel possibilities). Elon opened wide from the button, so checking OOP preserves chips, controls the pot, and lets him bluff or barrel weaker parts of his range. I’ll check to induce action; if Elon fires a small probe bet I might call one street to see a cheap turn, but I will fold to significant pressure or a raise since I have very little equity and no showdown value.

Flop 8d9s2h is fairly dry rainbow board with no strong draws completed, though 98 could hit broadway connectors or sets. As preflop raiser from button, my range is wide and perceived strong. Minni checked BB after calling raise, suggesting weakness or traps but likely weak given her history of folding to postflop aggression. I have full position, deep stacks, and J4ds has backdoor flush draw and some wheel potential, plus live jack. Standard continuation bet ~60% pot denies equity and takes it down often. Betting 400 builds pot small while maintaining fold equity.

Elon opened wide from the button and I defended the big blind with 4♥5♠ to see a flop. The 8♦-9♠-2♥ flop completely misses me — I checked to control the pot and induce a probe. Elon now bets 400 into the 1,050 pot (small/standard c-bet). Calling 400 would commit 400 to potentially win ~1,850 (pot after my call), so I need ~21.6% equity. My hand’s immediate equity is low, but the bet is small and the button’s c-betting range is likely wide enough to justify seeing one more street. I’ll call one street to realize equity and fold to significant turn aggression (raise or a large bet). If I pair or pick up a meaningful draw I’ll continue; otherwise I’ll give it up to heavy pressure.

I hold 4♥5♠ on a Q♥ 9♠ 8♦ 2♥ board. I called the flop c-bet as a cheap look, but the turn Q pairs the board and doesn’t improve me — no pair, no real draw (flush/straight impossible with one street left and only one heart in hand). Betting OOP with nothing would be a thin bluff that’s unlikely to fold many of Elon's continuing hands and would bloat a pot I’m usually giving up. I’ll check to control the pot and fold to meaningful aggression; if Elon checks behind we get a free river to reassess, and if he fires a bet I’ll fold most of the time (only calling very small probes since I only have ~6 outs to a low pair).

Post-turn on a 8d9s2hQh board, heads-up vs Minni in BB who checked both streets after calling pre and flop c-bet. My Jd4d has backdoor diamond flush draw and gutshot potential with runner-runner wheel or broadway blockers (J blocks some straights). As button raiser/c-better, my range includes strong hands like sets, overpairs, AQ/KQ top pair, Qx two-pair, flush draws – turn Q hits many value combos. Minni's check-check suggests capped range: middle pairs, stubborn draws, or traps, but her history (folds to turn aggression in hands like 26,34,42) indicates fold equity. Pot 1450 deep (~190BB effective), betting ~60% pot (900) applies pressure, denies equity to her floats/draws, builds pot for river shove if check-called, and realizes max EV by often winning unimproved. Checking risks giving free card to improve/draw out or induces her value. Aggression maximizes stack long-term.

Elon bet 900 into a 2350 pot. Calling 900 would commit me to a 3,250 final pot, so I need roughly 900/3250 ≈ 27.7% equity to break even. With 4♥5♠ on 8♦-9♠-2♥-Q♥ I only have 6 outs to pair (≈13% to hit the river) and no straight/flush draw, so my raw equity is far below the price. Elon’s line (button open, flop c‑bet, larger turn bet) is consistent with a queen or other made hands and not bluff-heavy enough to justify a loose call. I checked the turn to control the pot and said I’d fold to meaningful aggression — this bet qualifies. Fold and preserve stack for better spots.

In a 3-handed game, T8o on the button is a standard opening hand to put pressure on the blinds. My stack is currently the shortest at the table (approx. 53 BBs), so I need to find opportunities to pick up blinds and antes without overcommitting. I will raise to the standard 3x to see if I can take the pot pre-flop or play in position.

I’m in the SB with J♦7♠ facing a button open to 300 (I’ve already posted 50, so 250 more to call). J7 offsuit is a marginal, often-dominated hand and plays poorly out-of-position. Calling 250 commits chips into a multiway pot (the BB still acts) with a hand that has poor post-flop playability and few blockers for a profitable 3-bet bluff. A 3-bet as a bluff is possible in general, but J7o is a weak candidate — it won’t fold out enough of the opponent’s range and has poor equity if called. With a healthy stack (≈140 BB) there’s no need to force a marginal spot now; preserve chips and wait for better position/hand. Fold.

Preflop in BB facing a button raise from Flash to 300 (to call 200 into 450 pot). Holding Qs5h offsuit, which is a weak hand with poor postflop playability out of position: not suited, not connected, queen high but trash kicker. Flash has a wide button range, but Q5o is dominated by many calling hands like Qx better, Ax, Kx, pairs, suited connectors. Pot odds ~2.25:1 not sufficient for such a speculative hand OOP. Deep stacked (200BB), no need to defend wide; fold to preserve stack for premium spots.

I’m on the button with a small pocket pair (3d3c). With 3s in position I want to both steal the blinds and preserve implied odds for set-mining if called. The table’s default opens are around 300, so I’ll open to a standard 3x-sized raise to apply pressure on the blinds and isolate the big blind (Flash ~53BB) while still keeping the price reasonable to call a 3-bet for set value. Plan postflop: play aggressively with a set, otherwise use position to control the pot; call reasonable 3-bets to set-mine but fold to overly large 4-bets or sizing that kills implied odds.

Preflop in SB with A9o facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB). Button range (Minni) is wide based on history of frequent raises with marginal hands. A9o blocks strong Ax hands, has good equity vs wide calling range, and postflop playability. OOP but 3betting isolates vs short BB (Flash 53BB), applies pressure, and takes initiative. Size to 900 (3x their raise) is standard for value/protection/fold equity. Calling OOP is suboptimal without position; folding too tight given pot odds (450:250 ~1.8:1) and hand strength.

I have been dealt pocket Kings (Kd Ks) in the Big Blind. Minni opened from the button and Elon 3-bet from the Small Blind. This is a premium hand and a perfect spot to 4-bet. My stack is around 53 Big Blinds, and the pot is already significant. I want to build the pot now and am prepared to go all-in if either opponent shoves. I will raise to 2400 to exert pressure and extract value.

I opened to 300 with 3♣3♦ intending to set‑mine or steal the blinds. Elon 3‑bet to 900 from the SB and Flash (short, ~3020) 4‑bet to 2400. To continue I must call 2100 into a 3600 pot — ~36.8% equity required. Pocket 3s only hit a set ~11.8% on the flop (~19% by the river), so calling purely to set‑mine is -EV. Implied odds are poor here because Flash is short (only ~620 left to extract if Elon folds) and Elon can have a strong or polarized 3‑bet/5‑bet range that could shove and force big decisions. 5‑bet/raising would be committing large chips with a hand that plays poorly multiway or versus a likely strong 4‑bet range. I’ll fold and preserve my stack for better spots.

Facing a large 4-bet from Flash in the BB (to 2400 or 24BB) after my 3-bet isolation over Minni's button open. A♥ 9♠ is offsuit, with only an ace blocker, and poor postflop playability OOP against a short-stack opponent who now has position. Flash's sizing (24BB into a ~9BB pot) screams strength (QQ+, AK likely), especially with 30BB effective behind (3020 stack). Pot odds ~2.4:1 require ~29% equity, but A9o has ~25-35% vs tight 4-bet range and dominates bluffs minimally. Calling commits me deep OOP with marginal equity; raising risks cooler or fold equity low vs committed villain. History shows Flash aggressive but this 4-bet sizing too strong. Deep stack (196BB), fold preserves chips for better spots over 150 hands.

Preflop on the button in a 3-handed pot with 5d9s offsuit, a weak gapped hand with poor playability. The pot is 150 and to call 100 into the BB, but SB likely folded (pot size suggests dead SB blind). Getting 1.5:1 odds requires too much equity against BB's wide defending range. No strong reason to raise as it's not a polarizing hand. Folding preserves stack for better spots, especially with deep effective stacks and tournament goal to maximize over 150 hands. Position allows wide range but 95o is too weak.

I am in a blind-vs-blind situation after Elon folded on the button. King-Jack offsuit is a premium hand in this context. To take control of the pot and apply pressure on Minni in the big blind, I will put in a standard open raise of 3x the big blind.

Facing a 3bb open from the small blind with K♦6♠ in the big blind, calling is the right, low-variance play. The SB’s open to 300 is a wide range; I get very good pot odds (call 200 into a 400 pot — total 300 committed) to see a flop and realize equity. K6o has decent equity vs a wide SB opening range and the stacks are deep enough to play post‑flop; I don’t want to create a bloated pot or run into a 3‑bet war by making a light reraise. Plan: call to see the flop, value or continue if I hit/top pair or a strong draw, and fold to heavy aggression on dry boards where I miss.

I have overcards with King-Jack, but the flop has come out monotone with three diamonds. I do not hold a diamond in my hand, meaning I have no redraws to a flush. This board texture is quite dangerous as many hands in Minni's big blind defending range will contain at least one diamond or even a made flush. I will check to see how the action develops and likely fold to a significant bet.

Board is T♦7♦4♦ — very wet with any two diamonds having a flush. I hold K♦6♠: one diamond (a blocker to some Kx♦ flush combos) and an overcard but no pair or real showdown value. Flash was the preflop raiser and checked the flop; I don’t want to inflate the pot with a marginal hand that’s often behind (many two‑diamond combos in his opening range). Check to control the pot, realize equity (can still hit a King or pair on turn) and see how he reacts. If he bets I’ll re-evaluate — call small bets with appropriate pot odds or fold to heavy aggression since I’m currently behind to most made hands here.

The turn has brought a fourth diamond to the board. I do not hold a diamond in my hand, which means any diamond in my opponent's hand now completes a flush. Given the board texture, my King-high has very little showdown value, and it's extremely risky to bluff into a board where the opponent is highly likely to have at least one diamond or is unlikely to fold even a low flush. I will check and see if Minni checks behind or bets. If she bets, I will fold.

I hold K♦6♠ on Td7d4d8d — that’s a king‑high diamond flush (the nut flush only loses to A♦). Flash opened wide from SB and checked flop and turn; his range contains many diamonds but also a lot of weaker hands. I want to extract value and deny a free river that could pair or bring a scary card, so betting is correct. A medium/large value bet (~2/3 pot) will get called by worse diamonds, single pairs and other hands that will pay to see the river while keeping pricing reasonable if he check‑raises. If he check‑raises very large I’ll re‑evaluate against a polarized range (A♦ or unlikely straight flush); for now we bet for value.

The board now shows four diamonds, and I do not hold a diamond in my hand. This means any diamond in Minni's hand currently makes a flush. Given the board texture and Minni's bet of 400 into a 600 pot, it is highly likely she has at least one diamond or is representing one. With only King-high and no diamond for redraws, I have zero reason to continue in this hand. I will fold.

I am on the button with A7o, which is a standard opening hand in a 3-handed game. My stack is about 63 big blinds, and while I am the short stack, I still have plenty of room to play. I will raise to 300 to take control of the hand and potentially take down the blinds immediately.

Stack and situation: I'm in the small blind (140BB) facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, to call 250). Q♦2♦ is a marginal, suited hand — it has some backdoor equity but is easily dominated by typical button opening ranges. A small 3-bet here as a bluff is weak (I lack good blockers and the BB can easily punish), and folding every time gives up too much equity vs a wide BTN open. The price and deep stacks make a defensive call the best option: I’ll call to 300 total to see a flop, and plan to fold to a sizeable 3-bet from Elon. Postflop I’ll proceed cautiously OOP — continue only with clear equity (top pair/strong draws) or good bluffing opportunities.

Pocket threes in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 and a small blind call. This is a standard set-mining spot. The pot is 700 and it costs 200 to call, giving about 3.5:1 direct odds, plus massive implied odds with effective stacks of 6k+ (Flash) and deep stacks overall. Button ranges are wide (Flash raises button frequently from history), and Minni calls loosely. Equity realization for 33 multiway is sufficient (~12-15% needed, we have more vs loose ranges). Folding premium small pair OOP here is too tight; 3-betting risks playing OOP with dominated hand postflop. Calling to realize equity and stack on set.

I hold Qd2d on 7s Qh 2c — that’s two pair (Queens and Deuces). This is a very strong hand on a relatively dry flop and I’m OOP to two opponents, so I should start extracting value and deny free cards. A half-pot bet (~450 into the 900 pot) charges single-pair hands (Qx, 7x, smaller pocket pairs) and protects against turn runners, while keeping worse hands and calls in. If I face a large raise I’ll re-evaluate (likely call a modest raise for value but fold to an extremely large/polarized 3-bet depending on reads).

Flopped bottom set with 3h3s on 7s Qh 2c board, an extremely strong hand in a multiway pot. Minni's SB call preflop vs button raise gives her a wide range: pairs (including overpairs like JJ+, AQ suited, suited connectors, Ax). Her flop bet of 450 into ~900 is polarized: value (sets, two pair, overpairs), thin value (QJ, KQ), or bluffs (missed draws, A-high). Calling is fine for pot odds (~4:1) but slowplays against her value range and misses value from worse. Raising builds the pot, extracts from overpairs/top pair/Two pair, charges draws (spade flush draw, gutshots like JT), and denies equity. Flash behind has 6k stack and wide button calling range (pairs, broadways), so raise may get called by worse or isolate if he folds. Board is fairly dry (one spade), low straight outs unlikely to beat us. Min-raise too small; sizing to ~3x her bet / pot-committing-ish vs Flash. Raise to 1400 total for value/protection, balanced size leaving room for stacks.

On this flop of Q-7-2, I have second pair with an Ace kicker. After the small blind (Minni) led out with a bet and the big blind (Elon) raised significantly, my hand strength is heavily diminished in a multi-way pot. Elon's raise into two players on such a dry board strongly suggests a Queen or better. Continuing would require committing a large portion of my stack with a marginal hand that is likely dominated. Therefore, folding is the most prudent play.

I hold Qd2d for two pair (Queens and Deuces) on 7s Qh 2c — a very strong made hand heads-up after Flash folded. I led for 450 to charge worse Qx/7x and small pairs; Elon’s raise to 1400 is sizable and shows strength, but his range still contains many hands I beat (Qx, some 7x, single pairs and drawing hands). Sets (77 or 22) are the main hands that currently crush me, but they are relatively few combos. I’m out of position, so I want to control pot size and avoid committing too much to a larger raise that simply polarizes and could be met with a shove. Pot is 2750 and it costs me 950 more to call (i.e. to have 1400 in the pot) — the price is reasonable given my equity and the likelihood of getting value from worse hands on later streets. I’ll call the 950 (total commit 1400) and re-evaluate on the turn: if the turn is safe I can extract more; if it brings a scary card or Elon's action becomes extreme I’ll proceed cautiously.

The J on the turn is a worrying card — it helps a lot of hands that beat my two pair. Elon’s line (calling preflop, raising my flop bet to 1400, then checking the turn) is ambiguous: it can be a pot-control line with a single Q or medium pair, OR a trapping line with sets/trips (77, 22, JJ) or QJ (which now makes him two pair). Rough combo count: 77 (≈3), 22 (≈3), JJ (≈6), QJ (≈6) — enough combos that beat me to be cautious. I’m heads‑up, out of position, and stacks are deep (my stack 12,390, pot 3,700). Betting here will fold out worse Qx hands and charge thin calls, but also risks a check‑raise that commits a large portion of my stack when I’m behind. Pot‑control is the prudent +EV line: check and see how Elon responds on the river. Plan: call reasonable river bets for thin value, fold to very large shoves or very aggressive turn/river lines that polarize to monsters, and value‑bet thinly only on safe rivers (or larger bets when I’m confident I’m ahead).

Turn Jd on 7s Qh 2c Jd board with bottom set of 3s remains an extremely strong hand, likely the nuts or close—no completed straights (possible T9 straight draw now open-ended, but thin), spade flush draw still live but dry board overall. Minni's line: loose preflop call vs button raise, flop bet into multiway pot (thin value Qx/overpair or bluff), then calling my raise suggests medium strength like QQ, QJ, JJ, TT, AJs or draw. Her turn check after calling flop raise is weak—either trapping slow with monster (unlikely given her aggression/history), showdown value afraid of further action, or missed draw giving up. Checking induces her bluff or extracts from weak value, but pot control unnecessary with huge equity (~90%+ vs her calling range). Deep effective stacks (~12k), betting builds pot for river value from worse (overpairs, uncoordinated Qx, two-pair like Q7/Q2 rare). Size ~2/3 pot denies equity to draws, gets called by worse, leaves room for river shove if villain stacks off. Opponent tendencies: Minni calls loosely/stationy in history (e.g., Hand 25 called down rivers), so value bet optimal over check.

I hold Qd2d for two pair (Queens & Deuces) on 7s Qh 2c Jd — a very strong made hand heads‑up but vulnerable to a limited number of hands (sets and QJ/Q7 two‑pair). Elon’s line (call preflop, raise my flop bet to 1400, then fire 2500 on the turn after I checked) is strong but still contains many worse hands (single Qx, KQ, KJ, some floats and bluffs) that I can get value from. Folding is too weak here; raising would polarize the pot and likely induce a shove from the hands that already beat me. Pot odds to call (~28–30%) are reasonable for two pair versus his range, and calling keeps worse hands and bluffs in. I’ll call 2500 to control pot size, extract value on rivers where appropriate, and re‑evaluate if Elon shows further aggression (fold to an extreme shove or call reasonably sized river bets for thin value).

I have Qd2d for a full house (Queens full of Twos) on 7s Qh 2c Jd Qs — effectively the nuts except for a few very specific Qc+J/Qc+7 combos. Elon’s line (calling PF, raising my flop bet, firing the turn) is aggressive and can contain hands that beat me, but those combos are rare; the majority of his range is behind. Pot is 8,700 and my stack is 9,890 — I should be extracting value rather than slowing down. A value bet of roughly half the pot (~4k) will charge worse hands and bluffs, keep weaker hands in, and still allow me to control the size of the pot if Elon shows extreme aggression. If Elon raises large or jams I’ll re-evaluate (but with this full house calling a normal sized raise is correct in most cases). Bet size chosen to maximize value while managing pot control and opponent reaction.

River Qs pairs the flop Qh, bringing two queens on board (7s Qh 2c Jd Qs). Bottom set of 3s remains extremely strong—likely best hand against most of villain's range. Minni's line (loose preflop call vs button raise, flop bet/call raise multiway, check/call large turn bet, now lead river bet for 4000 into 12700 pot) is consistent with medium queens like AQ/KQ/AJ (now trips), turned Jx two pair, or thin value/overpair that improved. Weaker hands (bluffs/draws like spades, JT gutshot) unlikely to barrel after check-calling 2500 turn bet. Better hands possible: QQ (set to full), 77/22 (sets to full), JJ/QJ/Q7/Q2 (full houses), but these are narrow vs her SB calling range (wide suited Ax, broadways, pairs). Her history shows calling stations (e.g., Hand 25 called down river multi-street, Hand 78 called river raise light), loose aggression but foldy to big bets. Pot odds ~3.2:1 direct (~24% equity needed, we crush her value range ~85%+ equity, even vs nuts range ~30% sufficient). She has only ~1890 behind (stack 5890 pre-bet), so call builds massive pot (~20k+), positions us to extract remaining if she has showdown value or overbets weak. Raising to 8000 risks folding out trips/Qx (max value) while getting snapped by nuts; overplays vs her capping range. Check-fold impossible with hand strength/depth. Call realizes full equity, invites stack-off from worse, tournament goal favors pot-building with nuts-ish hand deep (15k stack, 89/150 hands).

Button with Qc8h in a 3-handed spot: marginal but playable with great position and decent steal potential. I want to take the initiative and pressure the SB/BB — a standard 3BB open (to 300) gives good fold equity, keeps the pot manageable, and lets me play postflop in position against their calling ranges. If I face a large 3‑bet from Elon I’ll fold; if I get called I’ll c-bet favorable flops, float and realize equity when appropriate, and fold to heavy resistance. Raising to 300 maximizes EV for this spot over just limping/calling.

K2s is a marginal hand at best from the small blind facing a button raise from Minni, who has been aggressive in previous hands. Out of position against two opponents (Flash still to act in BB), the hand lacks the strength or playability to call or raise profitably. Folding preserves our stack for better spots in this multi-hand tournament.

I am in the Big Blind with a pocket pair of sixes. Minni, who is currently the chip leader, raised from the Button to 300. 66 is a strong hand in three-handed play and is ahead of a typical wide Button opening range. I have a stack of 59.2 BBs. Calling allows me to see a flop at a good price (200 to call into a pot of 450) and potentially hit a set, while keeping the pot manageable against an aggressive chip leader. Folding is not an option, and 3-betting might be unnecessary given the stack depths and the fact that 66 often plays well as a mid-strength hand post-flop.

I have pocket sixes on a paired board of 7-7-4. This is a relatively safe board for my hand, as I have an overpair to the 4 and only lose to a 7 or a higher pocket pair. However, as the first to act on the flop, checking is the most appropriate play. It allows me to see how Minni, the pre-flop aggressor and chip leader, reacts. If she bets, I can comfortably call a standard continuation bet given the strength of my hand relative to a wide button opening range. If I bet now, I risk folding out her bluffs while getting called primarily by hands that beat me.

I opened from the button and was called; the flop is 7d 7h 4c and Flash checked. With Qc8h I have no pair but I was the preflop aggressor and have position and range advantage. This is a fairly dry, paired board where a continuation bet will fold many unpaired overcards and weaker hands the BB defends with. I’ll make a medium-sized c-bet (~60% pot = 400) to extract folds and keep the pot manageable if called. Plan: if he folds I take the pot; if he calls I proceed cautiously on the turn and only continue barreling if I pick up equity (a Q or club) or the turn card favors my range; if he raises I’ll fold — I have no made hand or profitable call vs a raise here.

On this dry, paired board of 7-7-4, my pocket sixes represent a strong hand, acting as an overpair to the 4. Minni, as the chip leader and pre-flop aggressor, is expected to continuation-bet here with a very wide range, including many air hands and high-card combinations. Raising now would likely only force out her bluffs and get me called by hands that beat me (7x or 88+). By calling, I keep her range wide and can re-evaluate on the turn based on the card and her subsequent betting action. My goal is to catch bluffs and minimize loss against her likely aggressive style.

The turn brings the third 7, giving me a full house (Sevens full of Sixes). This is a very strong but polarized board. If Minni has a pocket pair higher than sixes (88-AA) or the case seven, I am significantly behind. However, in three-handed play, she is likely to be betting a wide range of hands. Checking is the most prudent move here, as it allows her to continue bluffing with air or betting with hands I beat (like a 4 or smaller pocket pairs), while keeping the pot manageable if I am indeed facing a superior hand.

Turn brings the 7s giving the board trip sevens (7d 7h 4c 7s). I hold Qc8h and have no pair, no realistic draw, and essentially zero showdown value. Flash called my 400 c-bet on the flop but checked the turn — they could be pot‑controlling with a marginal hand or slowly playing a very strong hand (a 7 or a 4). Betting here to bluff is high variance and low EV: many hands that called flop (7x, 4x, sets, paired hands) will not fold, and I don’t want to bloat the pot with nothing. With the biggest stack and only one opponent left, pot control is the correct line. I’ll check and fold to a bet; if Flash checks back I can reassess the river and only attempt a bluff if a safe, believable card comes.

On the river, the board has completed as 7-7-4-7-4. My pocket sixes provide a full house (Sevens full of Sixes), which beats the board's full house (Sevens full of Fours). This hand beats any player holding a 4, lower pocket pairs (22, 33, 55), or any non-paired high cards. I only lose to the case seven (Quads) or pocket pairs 88 through AA. Given that Minni checked the turn after I checked, it is less likely she holds a 7 or a monster hand, as an aggressive chip leader would often continue betting for value or protection. By checking the river, I am playing my hand as a bluff-catcher, allowing Minni to bet with her missed overcards or air, whereas a bet from me might only be called by hands that have me beaten. I will check and plan to call a reasonable bet.

The river (7♦7♥4♣7♠4♥) pairs the board into a full house on the board. I hold Qc8h with no pair, no meaningful blocker and essentially zero showdown value. Flash called my flop c-bet and then checked the turn — that line is consistent with either a very strong holding (a 7, a 4, sets/pairs) slowplaying or a marginal hand pot-controlling, but in either case a river bluff is unlikely to fold enough of his calling range to be +EV. Betting here would bloat the pot when I am almost always behind; the only hands I can credibly fold out are weak overcards that are not common given his flop call. With the biggest stack and plenty of chips to play with, I prefer pot control and avoid a low-expected-value bluff. I will check and concede to a bet if he fires.

3-handed on the button with 113BB effective stack (shallowest is Flash at 67BB). Q2o is within a profitable button opening range against blinds, as position allows controlling the pot postflop, and queen-high has decent blocker value and showdown equity when called. History shows opponents frequently defend wide in blinds but fold to continued aggression. Standard open to 3x BB steals blinds often or builds pot in position. Raising maximizes EV over folding.

I am in the Small Blind with Ten-Nine of hearts, which is a strong suited connector in a 3-handed game. Elon raised from the Button to 300. While I could 3-bet to take control of the hand, calling is a solid play here to see a flop and realize the high equity and playability of these suited connectors without bloating the pot out of position against a deep-stacked opponent. I need to commit 250 more chips to match the raise.

Td5d is a weak, low suited one‑card hand. Facing a button open to 300 and a small‑blind cold‑call, this is a three‑way pot and I’m out of position vs the button postflop. To call 200 into a 700 pot I need ~22% equity; in a multiway spot T5s’s real equity vs a button open range and a calling SB range is usually at or below that and I’ll be dominated by many T‑x and broadway hands. 3‑betting to 500 as a bluff is thin here — it will either get called by better hands or fold out only the very weakest holdings, and I don’t want to play a marginal, dominated hand deep postflop. With deep stacks and plenty of hands left, preserving chips and folding is the +EV line.

I have missed this flop completely with 10-high and no direct draws. The board features an Ace, which is much better for Elon's range as the pre-flop raiser. Being out of position, the most prudent play is to check and see how Elon reacts. Leading out here would likely be a waste of chips against his range advantage.

Flop is Ac-7c-2s, an ace-high board with a club flush draw. Flash defended SB wide preflop and checked, showing potential weakness or a trap, but history indicates frequent check-folds to button aggression (e.g., Hands 51, 73, 79). Q2 gives bottom pair with queen kicker, decent showdown value vs bluffs/draws in calling range, blocks some 2x combos. No club, so vulnerable to flush draws, but position allows control. Effective SPR low (~9x pot), c-bet small to deny equity, extract value from worse (worse pairs/draws), and fold to raise. Half-pot sizing balances value/protection/protection vs floats. Maximizes EV over check (gives free card/draws) or larger bet (overplays weak pair).

I have completely missed this flop with Ten-high. The board features an Ace, which is very favorable for Elon, the pre-flop raiser from the button. I have no pair and no real draw (only extremely weak backdoor possibilities). Calling here would be a 'float' with zero equity, and raising into an Ace-high board against the pre-flop aggressor is unnecessarily risky. Folding is the most disciplined and mathematically sound play.

I am on the button with Ten-Nine of spades, a strong speculative hand that plays very well in position. Opening for a standard 3x raise is the correct play here to take control of the pot and capitalize on positional advantage against the blinds.

4♦9♥ is a very weak, offsuit hand that’s almost always dominated by a button open range. Flash opened to 300 and Elon (BB) is still to act — calling from the small blind will leave me out of position postflop and likely multiway against better hands. To call I’d need to commit a total of 300 (top up 250) into a 450 pot for poor equity; a light 3-bet to 500 is also unattractive because the BB can squeeze or call and I’ll still be OOP with a weak holding. With deep stacks we should be selective; conserve chips and wait for a better spot. Fold.

J8s suited connectors in BB facing button raise to 300 (SB folded). Pot 450, call 200 more for great odds (~2.25:1). Button range wide on BTN, this hand has good playability post-flop with flush/straight potential and implied odds given deep stacks (mine 11k, villain 6k). Folding too tight, 3-bet too strong. Call and realize equity OOP.

Flop is AA7 rainbow, extremely dry paired board. My J8s has no pair, no straight draw, and only a weak backdoor spade flush draw (one spade on board matches one of mine). Equity is very low against villain's button c-bet range, which includes many Ax, pocket pairs, bluffs, and sets. As first to act OOP with a hand that misses completely and lacks strong playability, checking is mandatory. Will fold to a bet, as pot odds won't justify calling without improvement, and check-raising is too thin without strong blockers or draws. Preserve stack for better spots.

On a paired Ace-high board (Ah-As-7c), as the pre-flop aggressor, I have a massive range advantage. It is very difficult for Elon to have an Ace after calling from the Big Blind, and many of his holdings will have to fold to a standard continuation bet. I will bet 250 (roughly 38% of the pot) to take the pot down now or define his hand.

Facing a 250 bet into 900 on a paired AA7 rainbow flop with J8s, which has zero equity (no pair, no draws beyond weak backdoors). Button's c-bet range on this dry board is strong: AA, Ax, 77s, bluffs. OOP call is -EV due to poor pot odds (250 to win 1150 ~4.6:1 but multi-street adjustment needed) and no playability. Consistent with preflop plan to fold to bet. Preserve stack for better spots in high roller.

Button in a three-handed game with T♥9♣ — a playable, connected hand with good postflop potential and strong positional advantage. Both opponents are deep enough to play postflop, so I want to open a wide button range to pick up blinds and play pots in position. A standard 3x BB-sized open (to 300) gives good fold equity, is consistent with the table’s sizing, and keeps my ranges balanced. Plan: if called I'll use position to C‑bet dry boards and control pot size on coordinated textures; if faced with a large, polarized 3‑bet (e.g. >> 700) I will let it go.

3-handed, button open very wide (~40-50% range). A8o has excellent equity (~43-45% vs typical BTN range) and blocks strong Ax hands like AA/AK. SB 3-bet light is standard to seize initiative, apply pressure OOP, and thin the field before BB acts. 3bet sizing to ~3x opponent's raise (900 total) is aggressive but appropriate at these depths. Folding too tight, calling risks multiway pot OOP with marginal hand.

I am holding 9h 2s in the Big Blind, which is a very weak hand. Minni opened from the button and Elon 3-bet from the small blind. Given the strength of my hand and the action ahead of me, there is no reason to commit more chips to the pot. I will fold.

I opened to 300 on the button with T9o and Elon (SB) 3‑bet to 900. T9o is a good hand to call in position against a standard 3x 3‑bet: it has straight/ Broadway potential, plays very well postflop, and we can use positional advantage to apply pressure or fold when appropriate. The 3‑bet size (900) is a standard, not an enormous polarized shove — with deep effective stacks and good implied odds calling is the correct, lower‑variance line. Pot is ~1300 and calling 600 (total commitment 900) requires ~32% equity; T9o should realize enough equity and postflop playability to justify seeing a flop. I don’t want to 4‑bet as a bluff with T9o (thin and opens us to tougher decisions or a shove), so I will call and play the flop in position.

Flop of 8d As 7h is an absolute dream with Ad8h: top two pair (aces and eights), very strong in 3-handed play. Minni called my preflop 3bet from button, so her range includes Ax (worse like AQo/K), sets (88/AA unlikely since I have A8), pocket pairs, suited connectors/draws. Board has spade flush draw and possible straight draws (e.g., 56, 9T, 49), so need to charge them heavily OOP while getting value from Ax/one pair. Pot is 1900; pot-sized bet extracts max value from worse hands without overbetting into nuts too thin. Protects against draws. My stack 10.5k effective vs her 20k.

I’m on the button with T♥9♣. Preflop I opened and called Elon's 3‑bet; flop A♠ 8♦ 7♥ and Elon bets 1,900 into a 3,800 pot. I have an open‑ended straight draw (any 6 or J = 8 outs, roughly ~31% to improve to river) plus occasional pair outs (T/9) that can pick up value sometimes. Pot odds: calling 1,900 into a 5,700 pot requires ~33.3% equity — my pure straight equity is slightly below that but adding pair outs, implied odds on a made straight, and the fact I’m in position makes a call the correct, lower‑variance play. Raising as a bluff is unattractive — Elon 3‑bet preflop and leads the flop, so he can have many strong hands and a raise would commit too much without extra equity. Folding is too conservative given the draw and my positional advantage, and I can always fold to heavy action on later streets if I miss. Plan: call to realize equity; if I hit the turn I will extract value (bet/raise as appropriate); if I miss and Elon barrels big I’ll let it go.

Top two pair (aces and eights) with Ad blocker remains extremely strong heads-up OOP after Minni called preflop 3-bet and large flop cbet. Her range: Ax (worse pairs/blocked AA), sets (77/88 unlikely), two-pair/draws that called big bet. Turn Qs completes T9 straight, improves Qx/AQ/KQ, adds second spade (possible flushes with two spades), but my hand blocks AQ/QQ/Aces. Board scary but favors my range. Must bet for value from worse (Ax, AQ, 87, Q8) and protection vs remaining spade draws/9x. Check risks free river to draws or her check-back weaker hands/rep. Size ~2/3 pot (3800) extracts max value, offers fold equity vs draws, commits ~44% stack (leaves ~4840), pot becomes 13300 if called for good SPR on river.

I opened and called the flop with T9o for the straight/paired outs, but the Q♠ on the turn missed me. Elon now bets 3,800 into a 9,500 pot — calling requires roughly 3,800 / (9,500+3,800) ≈ 28.6% equity. My clean straight outs (J or 6) are 8 cards (~17.4%); including pair‑outs (3 tens + 3 nines) brings raw outs to 14 (~30.4%), but many of those pair outs are dead versus the Ax‑heavy range Elon is representing (preflop 3‑bet + large flop/turn betting suggests AQ/AK/AA and other strong value hands). Raising as a semi‑bluff is possible, but the minimum raise to 7,600 is large and would either fold only marginal hands or get called/all‑in by top‑pair/two‑pair combos — leaving me drawing very thin. With a big stack advantage and a marginal draw against a polarized, value‑heavy opponent, folding and preserving my chips for better spots is the +EV play.

3-handed on the button with K7o, an excellent position. K7o is well within the standard opening range (around 50-70% of hands 3-handed) due to king high, playability, and ability to steal blinds or play profitably postflop in position. Blinds are loose/passive based on history (frequent calls, small bets). Deep stacks (143 BB effective vs Minni, shallower vs Flash), but pot odds favor raising for value/steal. Standard raise size 3x BB to 300, as seen in prior opens. No reason to limp or fold.

I am the short stack at the table with 66.7 big blinds. Holding 6h 5c in the Small Blind against a Button open from Elon, who has been active and has a significantly larger stack, is a clear folding situation. Calling out of position with a weak, offsuit connector is generally a losing play, especially with the Big Blind still to act, which could lead to a squeeze or simply being forced to play a multi-way pot out of position with a hand that rarely flops well. I'll preserve my stack for a better opportunity.

Elon opened to 300 from the button and Flash folded — it's heads-up. I have Qc3c in the big blind: a suited queen with a small kicker. With deep stacks (188bb) and Elon's frequent 300 opens, this hand is worth defending rather than folding or 3-betting. A 3-bet with Q3s is weak and often dominated; we don't want to inflate the pot out of position. Calling gives ~31% break-even pot odds (call 200 into 450) and Q3s has reasonable equity vs a wide BTN range plus flush/backdoor possibilities and a queen blocker. Plan: call to see the flop, continue on favorable queen/club/draw boards, and pot-control or fold to strong action on dry, high-card boards.

I defend Qc3c preflop for price and blocker value; on this flop (6s 9c 5h) I have no pair but queen-high and a backdoor club draw. The board is dry and uncoordinated — good for pot control. Elon is a frequent opener and will c-bet a wide range, so checking lets me realize equity, induce bluffs, and avoid bloating the pot out of position. Plan: check. If Elon fires a small-to-medium C-bet (roughly ≤ ~1/2 pot, ~300–350) I will call to see a turn and continue with any club/queen/board improvements; if he barrels large or raises heavily I will fold this very marginal holding unless the turn brings a direct improvement. If he checks back I keep the pot small and re-evaluate on the turn.

Heads-up on the flop with K7o after raising preflop from the button. Board is 6s9c5h, rainbow with some straight draw potential (e.g., 78) but not overly coordinated. No pair or draw, but king-high has showdown value and backdoor potential. Minni checked from out of position after calling pre, which suggests weakness or a trapping hand, but her history shows frequent check-folds to aggression (e.g., Hands 10, 14, 18, 26, 34). Deep stacks (140bb effective), pot 650. As preflop raiser in position, a standard continuation bet takes advantage of fold equity, protects against draws, and builds the pot when called with worse. Sizing to ~60% pot (400) balances value/thin value/semi-bluff, consistent with prior sizing (e.g., Hands 2, 15, 18). Checking gives up too much equity and initiative; betting maximizes EV long-term in 3-handed dynamic.

Preflop I defended Qc3c and checked the flop to control the pot. On 6s 9c 5h I only have queen‑high plus a backdoor club draw (needs both turn+river — ~3.3%) and ~12.5% to pair a queen by the river. Elon’s bet of 400 into a 1,050 pot gives me ~27.6% break‑even equity (400/(1050+400)); given his wide BTN opening/c‑betting range I expect enough showdown and improvement equity to continue for the price. Raising would be reckless with this hand and out of position. I will call 400 to see a turn and fold to heavy turn aggression unless I pair a queen or pick up a clear draw.

Heads‑up on the turn with Qc3c on 6s 9c 5h 2c. I called the flop to see the turn and the 2c completes a four‑to‑a‑club flush for me (Qc+3c + 9c+2c = one card to make a flush). I have 9 club outs to make the flush (~19.6% on the river) plus additional queen/trey outs to pair (~total ~32% to improve to at least a pair or a flush), so I have good equity. Elon checked the turn, which looks like weakness or pot control — a half‑pot semi‑bluff both picks up the pot now often and plays well if called. With the pot at 1450, a 725 bet gives the caller a ~25% break‑even threshold (725/2900) which is below my ~32% improvement-equity, so this sizing is +EV as a semi‑bluff. Plan: bet 725 to take it down now or build the pot with a strong draw; fold to a significant raise (I don’t want to go to war OOP with a one‑card draw unless odds are compelling), and re‑evaluate on the river if called.

Heads-up pot on turn with Kd7c rainbow king-high after preflop raise and flop cbet of 400 called by Minni (OOP). Board: 6s9c5h2c - low, somewhat drawy with wheel draws (34) completed and backdoor clubs/straights, but no strong improvement for me. Pot 2175, she leads 725 (~33% pot), sizing suggests possible thin value (e.g., 77-TT, 34 str8, 9x), protection (draws like 78,47, A4), or bluffs (missed draws, floats). Her range is wide from calling flop OOP: pairs <9, draws, Ax suited. King-high has ~18-25% equity vs that (blocks Kx value, live cards), and pot odds are excellent (725 to win 2900, ~20% needed). In position deep (100bb+ eff.), calling realizes equity cheaply, denies her free river cards, and keeps pot small for fold equity on river if she checks weak. Raising (min 1450) risks overcommitting to bluffs without fold equity vs value-heavy leads, folding loses too much EV given price. History shows Minni calls stations but folds to aggression sometimes (e.g., Hands 10,26); plan to call and evaluate river check-raise/value bet.

Board: 6s 9c 5h 2c Ts. My hand Qc3c — I missed the river (no flush, no pair) and only have queen‑high. On the turn I semi‑bluffed 725 and Elon called, which narrows his continuing range to lots of pairs (9x, 6x, 5x, 2x), some two‑pair/set combos and the occasional slower play. He now checks the river — either pot control with a real hand or giving up — so his fold equity is likely low. To make a profitable river bluff I'd need a fairly high fold frequency (e.g. a 1,000‑chip bluff needs ~26% folds, 1,500 needs ~34%); given he called the turn I don't expect him to fold that often. Betting here turns a marginal situation into a larger, negative‑EV confrontation; checking preserves chips and lets me realize showdown equity (and fold to river aggression). I check.

River board completes with Ts, a relatively blank card that brings possible 78 straights (6789T, but board is 2569T - 78 makes straight) and flush draws unlikely. My Kd7c is king-high, no pair/draw, but blocks top of range (Kx value hands). Preflop raise, flop c-bet called OOP, her turn lead 725 into 2175 (~33% pot) suggested value/protection (pairs 77-TT, 9x, 56/47 str8s/wheel), but checking river after villainous line indicates weakness, polarization to bluffs/missed draws (78dd, J8/A4 wheel), or trapping monsters unlikely given history. Her calling range flop OOP is wide (pairs <9, draws, Ax), but turn lead filters to medium strength. Pot 2900, deep effective ~100bb (mine 12915). In position, king-high has solid showdown value (~30-40% vs polarized range), excellent vs bluffs. Checking realizes equity for free, induces bluffs if she had check-raise plan (unlikely), avoids getting stacked by better (88+, sets, str8s). Thin value bet (e.g., 1500-2000) risks calls from worse only occasionally, but her stationy tendencies (calls river bets Hands 10,25,30,34,50) and fold to aggression sometimes mean EV neutral/negative. No fold equity needed; betting induces worse to fold too often or get called by better. History shows Minni checks weak rivers (Hands 8,19,29,33); check-behind maximizes EV long-term 3-handed.

I am on the button with King-Seven suited (Kd 7d). This is a standard opening hand in a three-handed game to put pressure on the blinds and potentially win the pot pre-flop or play a pot in position. I have about 67 big blinds, which gives me enough room to raise and play post-flop. I will open with a standard raise to 300.

I’m in the small blind with 5♠6♦ facing a 3x open to 300 from the button and the big blind still to act. Calling 250 would leave me out of position against at least one active raiser in a multiway pot with a hand that’s badly dominated and has poor showdown equity — I’d need ~36% just to break even on the call. A min 3‑bet bluff to 500 has limited fold equity here (button likely to continue) and would commit chips in a spot where I’m out of position postflop. Stack sizes and tournament life argue for pot preservation and waiting for a better spot, so I fold.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 from Flash after SB folds. My hand QcJc is a strong suited Broadway hand with great playability, straight and flush potential. Button ranges are wide, especially at 3x raise. Heads-up postflop I'd act first, but 3-betting takes initiative, builds pot with strong equity, and denies equity to weaker hands. Effective stacks deep (Flash ~64bb), good implied odds either way, but 3-bet sizing to 900 (3x) applies pressure while keeping fold equity vs wide button range. History shows Flash aggressive but folds to pressure often. Raising maximizes EV over calling or folding.

I opened with Kd 7d from the button, and Elon 3-bet from the big blind to 900. While K7s is not a monster, in a 3-handed match, it's a solid hand to defend in position. I'm getting good pot odds (600 to call into a pot of 1250) and have the advantage of being last to act on all future streets. I will call to see the flop and evaluate based on the board texture.

Preflop 3-bet with QJo suited was correct for initiative and playability. Flop 2h 2c Th is a paired, low board that misses most of Flash's calling range (wider button 3-bet calls like AJs+, KQs, 88-JJ, ATo+). My range hits harder with overpairs and sets. I have QJ overcards (live), backdoor club flush draw (with 2c on board), and backdoor straight potential. As first to act OOP, c-bet to continue representing strength, protect equity, deny free cards to draws (hearts, gutshots), and build pot when ahead. Sizing ~70% pot (1300 into 1850) balances value from worse pairs/draws and fold equity vs air. Flash folds often to postflop aggression per history (e.g., Hands 51,55,56). Check-fold too weak given initiative; check-raise risks overplaying.

On a board of 2h 2c Th, after calling a 3-bet pre-flop, I have King-high with no direct draws and only a very thin backdoor diamond possibility. Elon has fired a continuation bet of 1300 into a 3150 pot. Given that Elon's 3-bet range frequently contains overpairs (JJ-AA), Tens (AT, KT, QT, JT), or strong Aces that currently have me crushed, King-high is not strong enough to continue. While I am in position, floating here without even a gutshot or a flush draw against an aggressive opponent is likely to lead to a difficult turn decision. I will fold and preserve my remaining 57 big blinds for a better spot.

Three-handed on the button Q♠6♥ is marginal but well within a button opening range — we want to take the initiative and either steal the blinds or play a single-raised pot in position. Standard sizing here is about 3x the BB (300 total): big enough to apply pressure to Flash in the BB (who has ~56BB) and to prevent free flops, but not so large that we commit too much facing aggression from Elon in the SB. If either opponent 3-bets big I’ll fold Q6o; if called I’ll play position postflop, c-betting favorable textures and pot-controlling when behind. Raise to 300 to open the pot and keep options postflop.

86o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and connectivity. Facing a button raise from Minni, who raises frequently but can have a wide range, folding from the small blind out of position is standard. Pot odds are about 1.8:1 (calling 250 into 450), but equity against Minni's likely button opening range (20-40% of hands) is around 25-30%, not justifying the call OOP where postflop playability is terrible on most boards. Preserving stack for better spots in a tournament with 55 hands left and effective stacks deep except Flash short. No history specific to this spot warrants a hero call.

I am holding Queen-Ten of hearts in the Big Blind, which is a strong starting hand in a 3-handed game. Minni has opened from the button for 300. I am getting favorable pot odds (200 to call into a pot that will be 650) to see a flop. QhTh has excellent playability and post-flop potential. Given my stack size of approximately 57 big blinds, I want to defend my blind here rather than 3-betting, as I can play a wide range of flops comfortably against a standard button opening range.

On this King-high flop, I have missed completely. I have two overcards to the 8 and 5, and a backdoor heart draw, but overall this board favors Minni's button opening range which contains many Kings and pocket pairs. My position as the first to act is also a disadvantage. Checking is the standard play here to see how the pre-flop aggressor reacts. If Minni bets, I will most likely fold unless the bet is extremely small, as I have very little equity.

I opened preflop from the button and have position. The flop Kc–8h–5s is pretty dry and misses most of my broad raising range; Flash’s check shows weakness or a float. As the preflop aggressor I should c‑bet frequently to take down the pot and fold out many of his non‑K hands and overcards. I’ll make a modestly strong c‑bet (~60% pot) to apply pressure but keep the pot manageable if called. Plan: fold to a significant check‑raise; if he calls I’ll pot‑control on the turn unless I pick up equity or get a very favorable card to continue barreling.

On this K-high flop, I have missed completely with my Queen-Ten of hearts. I have two overcards to the 8 and 5, and a backdoor heart draw, but Minni's button opening range frequently contains Kings, pocket pairs, or stronger Ace-high hands. Minni's bet of 400 into 650 is a standard continuation bet that puts pressure on my weak range. I am currently the short stack and out of position; calling here with very little equity and only backdoor draws is not a profitable long-term strategy. I will fold and wait for a more favorable spot to commit chips.

Preflop on the button with 3d7h, an offsuit, unconnected, low-card hand with poor playability and little showdown value. Even in position with deep stacks (167 BB effective vs blinds), this is a clear fold against competent opponents like Flash and Minni, who have shown aggression in blinds in prior hands. No need to risk chips on a speculative hand without suited/connectors or higher cards for stealing or implied odds. Preserve stack for better spots.

I am in the Small Blind with King-Ten offsuit (Kh Ts). Elon on the button has folded, leaving only me and Minni (Big Blind). KTo is a very strong hand in a blind-vs-blind situation. Raising to 300 is a standard open to take the initiative and potentially win the blinds or build a pot where I likely have the better hand.

Facing a 300 open from the SB with Q♦5♥ in the BB. Pot is 400 and it’s only 200 to call; effective stacks are deep (I: 17715 / Flash: 5170). Q5o is a marginal hand but worth defending the big blind against a likely wide SB opening range. I don’t want to 3-bet out of position with a dominated hand and I don’t want to fold a lot of equity for just 200 into 400. Plan: flat-call 200 (total commitment 300), see the flop, and play cautiously OOP — fold to big 3-bets or heavy postflop aggression without improvement, but continue or apply pressure if I pair the Q or pick up a usable draw.

I am the pre-flop aggressor and the flop of 2h-8s-2d is extremely dry, favoring the range of the initial raiser. Holding King-high with overcards to the board, I have a significant equity advantage over much of the Big Blind's calling range. A standard continuation bet of 50% pot should be enough to take the pot down immediately or force Minni to fold weaker high-card hands and low pairs that might be uncomfortable on this texture.

Call 300. I defended the big blind preflop with Q♦5♥ intending to see a flop and play cautiously out of position. On 2♥‑8♠‑2♦ I have no made hand and only a backdoor heart — but Flash’s small c-bet (300 into a 900 pot) looks like a wide continuation range and is cheap to call. Calling realizes equity and lets me see the turn without committing a large portion of my stack. Plan: fold to significant turn aggression if I don’t improve, continue or raise only if I pair the Q/5, pick up a strong draw, or get a clear read that Flash is bluffing.

On a double-paired board of 2h-8s-2d-8c, the texture is extremely polarized. I was the pre-flop aggressor and bet the flop, and Minni called. The second 8 on the turn is a significant card; it counterfeits any pocket pairs lower than an 8 (like 33-77) and gives any 8 or 2 a full house. While I have King-high, which has some showdown value against her complete air, it is unlikely to get called by worse if I bet again. By checking, I control the pot size and can evaluate her reaction. If she checks behind, I may still have the best hand or can see a cheap river. If she bets, I can decide based on her sizing whether to continue or fold, preserving my 48.7BB stack.

Check. The turn (2h 8s 2d 8c) is a very dangerous, double‑paired board: any hole 2 or 8 now has trips and can easily check‑raise or slow‑play. Flash’s line (SB open, small flop bet then check on the turn) is consistent with both strong hands and pot‑control; it’s also the exact spot I don’t want to bluff‑raise into. I have Q♦5♥ with no pair and no meaningful draw, so betting is a pure bluff that risks either a check‑raise or a call from hands that already beat me. I’ll check to control the pot size and see the river; unless I pair or pick up a clear read, I’ll fold to significant river aggression.

The river Ten improves my hand to two pair (Tens and Eights) with a King kicker. The board is now 2-8-2-8-T. Anyone holding an 8 or a 2 has a full house, but the check-check on the turn makes it less likely that Minni has an 8. Since I have a pair of Tens, I beat all Ace-high hands and pocket pairs from 33-77 (which would play as 8s and those pairs). I am betting for value, expecting to be called by weaker pairs or Ace-high hands that may be suspicious of the turn check.

Fold. The river (2h 8s 2d 8c Td) is a very unfavorable texture for Q♦5♥: the board already contains two pairs, so any 2 or 8 in Flash’s hand is a full house and beats my two pair, and any A/K/J/T or a mid-high pocket pair in his range will beat my Q‑kicker.
