My AdQh is pure ace-high with no pair, no flush draw (Ad blocks some AT but not much else), and gutshot straight draw at best (to Q for broadway but unlikely). Equity is low against value range (~20-30% vs typical caller), and even worse if villain checks back floats. OOP, betting as a bluff risks getting called by better and pot committed; checking allows pot control, realizes equity when ahead vs pure air (which villain might check), and folds to bets profitably. Long-term EV positive to check-fold here given dynamics and deep stacks.

On a paired board of 10-6-10, the range of the Big Blind often contains more 10s and 6s than the Small Blind's opening range. Elon has checked to me, which could indicate a weak hand or a slow-play with a monster. I have a backdoor club draw and some marginal showdown value. Since I have potential to improve and there's no immediate reason to turn my hand into a bluff on this specific board texture, I will check behind to see a free turn card and realize my equity.

Preflop raise with AdQh was standard from SB. Flop Td6cTs was unfavorable for our range—paired board favors BB's calling range (Tx, pairs, straight draws)—so checking OOP for pot control was correct; villain's check-back suggests possible weakness, floats, or traps. Turn Kh brings a card that improves villain's likely holdings (KT for two pair, AK for top pair, QJ for gutshot completing to Broadway potential), while our ace-high has no pair, no flush draw (only incidental Qh to Kh), and minimal straight outs. Equity remains low (~25-35% vs adjusted villain range post-check). Deep stacks (pot 600, our stack 13.6k) favor checking: pot control OOP, realize equity when ahead vs air/bluffs (villain checked flop), fold profitably to bets, and avoid bloating pot with marginal hand. Betting as bluff is -EV here—villain calls wide with Tx/draws, raises monsters; thin value unlikely with ace-high. Check to showdown or face river decision maximizes EV long-term.

On the turn, the King of Hearts is a card that generally favors the pre-flop aggressor's range, yet Elon has checked for the second time. By checking the flop (10-6-10) and now the turn (K), Elon's range appears capped, likely consisting of mid-pairs or Ace-high hands that are looking for a cheap showdown, or a complete 'give up'. While I could attempt to bluff here representing the King or a Ten, checking behind allows me to realize my equity for free. My 9-high has almost no showdown value, but a 9 or 7 on the river could potentially give me the best hand against his 'air' or allow for a more convincing bluffing opportunity depending on the river card. I will check to see the river.

River 4d is a blank that doesn't change the board significantly—no flush completes (two diamonds but no flush draw obvious), no new straights, low card. Our AdQh is ace-high with backdoor diamond blocker and queen for broadway blocker, but no made hand. Villain's BB calling range vs our SB raise includes Tx (ATs, KTs, QTs, JTs), small pairs, suited connectors (87s, 76s for str8 draws), weaker Ax suited. Flop paired Ts favors villain's range heavily (Tx hits hard), and his check-back suggests possible weakness (missed draws, weak Tx trapping, air floats). Turn Kh likely improves some (KT two-pair, AK/AQ top pair, QJ str8 draw), but another check indicates caution—no nuts betting for value. Deep stacks (pot 600 vs 13k+), OOP: betting thin value with ace-high risks calls from all Tx and better (sets, two-pair) while bluffs get called wide; villain can hero-call profitably. Checking realizes equity: wins vs missed draws/bluffs (villain checked twice OOP), chops some better hands, pot control avoids spew. Long-term EV-max: check to showdown with showdown value ace-high likely top ~20-30% of villain's range post-check-check.

Elon has checked for the third consecutive street after being the pre-flop aggressor. On a board of T-6-T-K-4, his triple check strongly indicates a capped range, likely consisting of weak Ace-high hands or low pocket pairs that are hoping for a cheap showdown. My 9-high has no showdown value and will almost certainly lose if I check. By betting 400 into the 600 pot, I am representing a Ten that I slow-played or a King that I checked on the turn for pot control. Given the passivity Elon has shown this hand, this sizing is sufficient to fold out his 'air' and most of his marginal one-pair hands.

Facing a delayed river bet of 400 into 600 after mutual checks on flop and turn on a paired Td6cTs flop with Kh turn and 4d river. Board favors BB's wide defending range heavily with paired tens, and villain's check-backs suggest caution but river bet reps Tx (trips or two-pair like KT/KTs), improved Kx (AK, KQ), or thin value. AdQh is pure ace-high, blocking some AK/AT but beating only bluffs/missed draws which are underrepresented in betting range post-check-check (villain doesn't bluff much OOP after two checks). Pot odds require ~28.6% equity (400 to win 1000), but vs value range we have ~10-20% (loses to all pairs of T/K). Bluff raise risky deep-stacked (13k effective) as villain calls wide with medium strength. Folding preserves stack for tournament survival/max EV, consistent with prior pot control strategy.

3-handed on the button with deep stacks (~136BB), J4 suited is well within my opening range (suited, connected, good postflop playability for straights/flushes). First to act preflop, standard to raise for value/steal. Frequent open size from history is 300. Min-raise to 200 is too small; 300 (3x) is standard and builds pot nicely without overcommitting.

In a 3-handed game, Ace-Eight offsuit is a strong enough hand to defend the Small Blind against a Button raise, especially against a player like Elon who opens frequently. While calling is an option, 3-betting (raising) here allows me to take the initiative, potentially win the pot pre-flop, or isolate Elon if the Big Blind folds. Given Elon has shown a tendency to fold to 3-bets in previous hands, a raise to 1000 is an effective way to put pressure on his wide button opening range while maintaining a strong hand if called.

Situation: 3-handed, I'm BB with A♥T♥. Elon (BTN) opened to 300 and Flash (SB) 3-bet to 1000; pot is 1400 and I must call 900 more (total 1000) or raise (min to 1700). With deep stacks (~138bb) A♥T♥ is a very playable hand but a marginal 4-bet candidate: a small 4-bet to 1700 gives little fold equity and a larger 4-bet commits me to a big pot out of position facing ranges that often dominate me. Calling keeps the pot manageable, realizes equity (ATs performs well vs a 3-bet range and has good playability on A/heart flops), and lets me see how Elon reacts — if he folds it will often be heads-up vs Flash; if he calls we can proceed cautiously multiway. Pot odds are reasonable (900 into ~2300 ≈ 39% break-even), so I’ll defend by calling to 1000 and make postflop decisions based on the flop texture and opponent actions.

Preflop on the button with Jd4d, a suited connector with excellent playability for straights, wheel, and flush draws. Deep stacks (~130BB) and position justify continuing with speculative hands against a 3-bet from SB (Flash, aggressive 3-bettor per history) and flat from BB (Minni, calls wide). Folding loses too much EV from position/implied odds. 4-betting is too thin and risks going multiway or getting 5-bet. Calling offers great pot odds (700 to call into 2300, ~4:1 implied), high postflop SPR (~40), and ability to realize equity heads-up or multiway. Plan: play postflop aggressively on favorable boards, control pot otherwise.

On this flop of Jh Qs Ks, the board is extremely coordinated and hits the calling ranges of both Minni and Elon very hard. My Ace-Eight offsuit has very little equity here beyond a gutshot straight draw (needing a Ten). Since I am out of position against two opponents who both called a 3-bet pre-flop, a continuation bet is unlikely to succeed and would be very expensive. Checking is the best play to see how the other players react to this heavy Broadway board.

The flop J♥ Q♠ K♠ gives me A♥ T♥ the Broadway straight (A-K-Q-J-T) — effectively the nut on this runout. Very few hands beat me right now (only a made spade flush if someone holds two spades and the board pairs with a spade runout, or another A‑T for a chop). Preflop action (BTN opened, SB 3‑bet, both called/called) indicates both opponents have fairly strong ranges (AK/AQ/KQ, pairs, suited broadways, some spade combos) — many of which will pay to see a flop card but also have spade equity. With two opponents I want both value and protection: a bet charges spade draws and extracts from worse made hands (Kx, Qx, Jx, pocket pairs, T9, etc.). I choose a ~2/3 pot sizing (2000 into the 3000 pot) — big enough to charge draws and get called by thin value, but not so huge that it folds out too much or overcommits me facing a big check‑raise. Plan: if called I’ll reevaluate the turn (continue for value/protection if safe); if raised I’m inclined to continue (I’m ahead of sets/two pair and most single‑card draws) but will be more cautious if the turn brings a spade that completes obvious flushes.

Button with Jd4d on JhQsKs flop, giving top pair with disastrous 4d kicker. Preflop, we got in multiway after my open-3bet-call dynamic (Flash 3bet aggressive, Minni wide caller from BB). Pot 5000, Minni donk-bets 2000 into checked pot after Flash (SB) checks – strong line from Minni, repping KQ, KT, AT, AJ+, QQ+, KK+, sets, or 10x straight. My hand crushed by any K/better J/Ax; even draws have fold equity but board favors her range heavily. Two spades on board add flush draw pressure. Calling 2000 (~40% pot) multiway kills SPR/implied odds (stacks deep but realization poor); raising thin/bluffs risky vs Minni's history of trapping/raising pots. Folding high-EV: preserves ~126BB deep stack for better spots in 3-handed tournament, avoids cooler potential (e.g., her AK/AQ crushes). Plan preflop was speculative playability, but this texture denies equity effectively.

On a board of Jh-Qs-Ks, the texture is incredibly heavy with Broadway cards that hit a 3-bet calling range very hard. Minni called a 3-bet from the Big Blind pre-flop and is now leading out for 2,000 into the pot after I checked. My Ace-Eight offsuit has very little equity here; while I have a gutshot straight draw to the nuts (needing a Ten), the pot odds (2,000 to win a total pot of 7,000) do not justify a call with only four outs (approx. 8.5% chance to hit on the turn). Furthermore, the board is coordinated for flushes and two-pair combinations which Minni is very likely to have given her line. Folding is the most disciplined and mathematically correct play here to preserve my stack.

In 3-handed play, a King-high hand on the button is generally strong enough to open for a raise. K4o is within a standard opening range for this position. I am raising to 300 to exert pressure on the blinds and take advantage of my position for the rest of the hand.

I’m in the small blind with 3♠8♣ facing a button open to 300 (I must call 250 into a 450 pot). This is a very weak, unsuited, unconnected hand that will be largely dominated by a typical opener. I’d be out of position postflop and Elon (BB) still has to act, so I risk a 3‑bet or a multiway pot with almost no playability. Pot odds (~28%) don’t justify calling against the raiser’s range and a bluff/raise here is unnecessary with my stack. Fold and wait for a better spot.

J4 suited is a weak hand preflop, especially out of position in the big blind facing a button raise from an aggressive player like Flash. The pot odds (calling 200 into 450) require about 31% equity, but J4s has poor playability OOP against a wide button range. No strong draws or blockers for bluff-raising. Deep stacks with 35 hands left; better to preserve stack and wait for stronger spots to maximize long-term EV.

Button with Jc6c: suited jack gives decent playability in position and is a hand I can open widely vs the blinds. Both opponents have shown aggression but also many folds to raises from position. I want to take the initiative and apply pressure while keeping the pot manageable with deep stacks — a standard 3xBB open is appropriate. I will raise to 300 total. Plan: take the lead postflop — c-bet favorable dry/J- or club-backdoor flops, check/control on heavy coordinated boards. If I face a sizable 3-bet, J6s is usually behind so I will fold to significant 3-bets; continue only vs very small, isolating 3-bets with clear pot odds and exploitative reads.

Pocket 2s is a speculative hand but excellent for set mining with deep stacks (124BB effective). Minni's button raise to 300 (3x) is likely wide, including many hands 22 crushes or has live cards against. Calling 250 more (total 300) into 450 pot gives ~1.8:1 direct odds, but massive implied odds if set hits (opponents have 114-156BB). Flash in BB may call wide, creating multiway pot ideal for small pairs. Folding too tight preflop; 3-betting risks isolation with marginal hand or bloating pot OOP. Prefer cheap flop to realize equity.

I am holding Pocket Tens in the Big Blind. Minni opened from the button and Elon called from the small blind. Pocket Tens is a premium hand in a three-handed game and is likely the best hand currently. By raising (3-betting), I can take control of the hand, potentially win the pot immediately, or build value against wider button-opening ranges and capped small-blind calling ranges. I will raise to 1200 to put pressure on my opponents and discourage them from seeing a cheap flop with speculative hands.

I opened to 300 with Jc6c which I’ll play in position, but Flash’s 3-bet to 1200 (pot 1800, to-call 900) is a large, significant 3-bet. Elon (SB) already called, so calling would create a multiway pot where J6c has poor equity and is frequently dominated by the 3-bettor’s range (pocket pairs and broadway/Ax combos). A light 4-bet bluff to 2100 is thin and not worth risking a large portion of my stack here. My pre-stated plan was to fold J6s to significant 3-bets — that’s the prudent, +EV line: preserve stack and wait for a better spot in position.

Pocket 2s is still a solid set-mining hand heads-up against Flash's BB 3-bet. Direct pot odds are 900 to call into 2700 (3:1, needing ~25% equity), which 22 achieves against a typical BB 3-bet range (QQ+, AK, some bluffs/broadways). Implied odds remain excellent with 90+BB effective stacks behind; Flash has shown aggression but also check-folds flops often. Button folded, so no multiway complication. Raising has poor equity realization OOP and fold equity is low vs BB strength. Folding is too tight given depths and history of profitable calls with small pairs (e.g., Hand 46). Call to flop and play postflop cautiously, folding to c-bets unless set or good draw.

Heads-up OOP vs Flash's BB 3-bet range after calling a button open. The flop Kc Kh 8d is a paired kings board that smashes villain's strong pre-flop range (KK+, AK, QQ+, broadway), giving them frequent two-pair, trips, or better. Pocket 22 has no showdown value, minimal equity (~5-10% vs polarized range including bluffs), and poor implied odds due to reverse implied odds on overpair boards. No draws, no hits for set-mining success. Betting risks raising into monsters or getting check-raised; fold equity minimal OOP vs strong range. Checking preserves stack, allows folding to likely c-bet. Continuation too loose given history of Flash value-betting strong flops (e.g., Hand 51 KK bet). Prioritize survival and stack maximization over speculative play.

On a paired board of EIGHT OF DIAMONDS, KING OF CLUBS, and KING OF HEARTS, my Pocket Tens are a strong hand but vulnerable to any King or a slow-played Eight. Elon checked to me after calling my pre-flop 3-bet. I will check behind for pot control. This allows me to keep the pot small in case Elon has a King, while also keeping his bluffs in the hand. If he has an Ace-high or a lower pocket pair, I am currently ahead, and checking allows me to evaluate the turn before deciding on further value betting.

Heads-up OOP vs Flash's BB 3-bet range on a Kc-Kh-8d-3h board. Pocket 22 missed completely for set-mining; equity remains dismal (~5-10%) against villain's strong polarized range (KK/AA/AK top of range, QQ+ overpairs, some bluffs). Flop checks were standard for pot control and equity denial, but villain's check-behind could be trapping monsters (slowplayed KK) or medium strength fearing check-raises. Turn 3h is a low blank that doesn't improve us and may complete weak gutshots or missed draws, slightly favoring villain's range. With 2700 pot and ~100BB effective stacks, betting risks raising into nuts (KK has us crushed) or getting check-raised off equity; fold equity low OOP post-check. No reverse implied odds concern since no showdown, but no value or semi-bluff either. Checking preserves stack, induces bluffs on river if villain weak, or allows free river card. Prioritizes survival and long-term stack growth over spewing chips with air in tough spot.

Elon has checked to me twice on a board of 8d-Kc-Kh-3h after calling my pre-flop 3-bet. My pocket Tens are very likely the best hand, but betting into a board with two Kings carries the risk of running into a slow-played monster or a check-raise. By checking behind on the turn, I control the pot size and ensure I can reach a showdown relatively cheaply. This line also keeps Elon's bluffs in his range, potentially inducing a lead on the river that I can comfortably call.

River Qh completes heart flush draws (Kh, 3h, Qh) and pairs any Qx holdings. Pocket 22 remains a complete airball with ~0% showdown value against Flash's BB 3-bet range (QQ+, AK, KQs, polarized bluffs). Double check-behind flop/turn on paired kings board suggests villain holds medium strength like QQ-JJ, AK (trips or two-pair), or missed draw/trap, but unlikely pure air given position and aggression preflop. Betting risks villain calling/raising with superior hands (high frequency) or check-raise; fold equity minimal (~10-20%) after passive streets OOP. Reverse implied nonexistent since no showdown planned. Checking maximizes EV by preserving stack (11290 deep, 117/150 hands), induces bluffs if villain weak (unlikely but free), or faces bet we happily fold. Consistent with prior pot control; no spew needed with brick in tough spot vs strong range. Prioritize survival/stack growth over hero calls or thin value.

Elon has checked for the third consecutive time on a board of 8d-Kc-Kh-3h-Qh. My pocket Tens remain a strong showdown hand, but the river Queen is a scare card that could have improved a portion of Elon's 3-bet calling range (like Ace-Queen). Given that he has checked three streets, it's unlikely he is trapping with a King, but it's also unlikely I'll get called by many worse hands if I bet. By checking back, I ensure I reach showdown and win the pot against his missed Ace-highs or smaller pocket pairs without risking a check-raise.

Preflop on the button with 32s, a weak suited connector. Position is good, but the hand has poor high card strength and is easily dominated. Deep stacks make speculative hands playable multiway sometimes, but limping is suboptimal, and raising with this hand risks playing OOP postflop with reverse implied odds if hit a low straight or flush. Opponents have shown wide ranges and aggression in history, but folding conserves stack for better spots late in tournament. Best EV to fold.

I am in the Small Blind with King-Seven suited. Since Elon has folded the button, this is a blind-vs-blind situation. K7s is a well-above-average hand for this scenario. I will put in a standard open-raise to 300 to take the initiative against Minni in the Big Blind.

Flash opened to 300 from the small blind. I’m in the big blind with A♦T♣ — a decent heads-up hand but marginal out of position. With deep stacks (~155bb) and only one opponent, I prefer to keep the pot manageable and realize equity rather than 3-bet a hand that can be dominated by stronger aces. Calling keeps my range wide, gives good pot odds to see a flop, and lets me evaluate the board before committing more. I’ll call the 300 (putting in 200 more) and proceed postflop based on texture and opponent action.

I have bottom pair with a King kicker on a coordinated board of Jack-Ten-Seven. As the pre-flop aggressor, I will place a standard continuation bet of half the pot. This allows me to maintain the initiative, protect my hand against various overcards that Minni might be holding, and extract value from potential draws or weaker hands that might call.

I have A♦T♣ on Td–Jc–7s: second pair with the best possible kicker versus many hands that call/raise pre. Flash’s flop bet is small (~300 into a ~900 pot), indicating a wide C-bet range and giving me very good pot odds to continue. Out of position and with a vulnerable hand (can be outkicked or dominated by Jx, or beaten by sets/pocket Jacks), I don’t want to inflate the pot with a marginal raise or get committed on later streets. Calling keeps the pot controlled, realizes my equity, and lets me re-evaluate on the turn (where I can proceed aggressively if the Ace or a blank arrives or if villain shows weakness). Therefore I’ll call the 300.

The turn 8c is a very dangerous card as it completes a straight for any hand containing a nine and coordinates the board significantly (7-8-10-J). My bottom pair of sevens has become much more vulnerable. Given that Minni called both pre-flop and on the flop, she likely has a piece of this board or a draw that has now improved. I will check to control the pot size and see how she reacts to this board texture.

I hold A♦T♣ on Td–Jc–7s–8c: top pair with the best kicker. Flash showed preflop aggression and c‑bet on the flop but checked the turn — that looks like range weakness rather than a value trap, so I should start extracting value and protect against the few turn/river straights (9x). The pot is only 1,200 and stacks are deep, so a half‑pot turn bet prices in value from worse tens, Jx, and medium pairs while keeping the pot controlled. I’ll bet ~600 to get called by worse and fold out pure bluffs; if I’m raised I’ll re‑evaluate or fold depending on sizing, and if called I’ll plan to re‑assess on the river.

On the turn, the 8c creates a highly coordinated board (7-8-10-J), where any 9 now makes a straight. Minni called pre-flop and called my continuation bet on the flop (J-T-7), which strongly suggests she has a piece of this board, such as a Jack, a Ten, or a straight draw. My bottom pair of sevens is now extremely vulnerable to almost her entire range. While I am getting 3:1 pot odds to call, I have very few clean outs to improve, and I would likely be forced to fold to any further aggression on the river. Given the high probability that I am already behind a Ten, a Jack, or a straight, I will fold now to preserve my stack.

On the button in a 3-handed game, I have a wide opening range, but 8-6 offsuit is near the bottom of that range. Given that Minni (SB) is the chip leader and has shown a willingness to play back at raises, and Elon (BB) is relatively solid, I will fold this marginal hand to avoid unnecessary variance and preserve my stack for better opportunities in the final stages of the tournament.

I’m in the small blind with Q♥7♣ and Flash folded, so it’s effectively heads-up vs Elon in the big blind. Pot is 150 and I only need 50 to call. With deep stacks (~160 BB) and heads-up dynamics, my open-raising range should be wide — Q7o is playable and benefits from initiative. I’ll make a standard min-open to 200 total to seize the lead and generate fold equity while keeping the pot controllable. If Elon calls I’ll c-bet favorable flops and exercise pot control on bad boards; if he 3-bets big I’ll re-evaluate (usually fold to very large 3-bets, consider calling smaller ones depending on odds).

Preflop in the big blind facing a small raise to 200 from the small blind (Minni) after button folds. My hand K♦4♥ is offsuit king-high. Pot is 300, calling 100 more (pot odds ~4:1). Minni's SB raise range is very wide given position and sizing, including many weaker hands, Ax, suited connectors, etc. K4o blocks some of her strong Kx and has ~35-45% equity against a loose SB opening range. Postflop OOP, I'll play cautiously: check-fold weak boards, check-call/raise if K or good draw hits. Folding wastes BB equity with great price; raising is overplay with marginal hand. Deep stacks allow postflop maneuvering. Defending wide maximizes EV long-term.

I opened preflop to 200 from the SB and Elon (BB) called, leaving a 400-chip pot. Flop: Jc 4s 5c and I hold Q♥7♣ — no pair but I have the initiative as the preflop raiser. This board is relatively dry (one high card and two low cards) and hits the caller’s wide defending range only partially, so a continuation bet will often take it down or charge draws. I’ll make a half-pot c-bet (200): large enough to fold out some unpaired broadways and weak Ax hands and to charge any real draws, but small enough to keep the pot controllable with a marginal hand given deep stacks. If Elon jams/raises big I’ll fold; if called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and look to value-bet only when I improve or to check and pot-control against aggression.

Preflop, we defended wide in the BB against a small SB raise from Minni, who has a very wide opening range from SB (offsuit Ax, suited connectors, broadway, pairs). Flop Jc4s5c hits our 4h for bottom pair with live K♦ kicker. Board is draw-heavy (clubs flush draw, gutshots like 36 or 23 OESD combos), but Minni's small 1/2 pot bet (200 into 400) suggests a polarized range: strong made hands (JJ,44,55,Jx, sets unlikely pre), weak draws/probes, or floats. Our hand has good blocker to 4x (her likely holdings), blocks some Kx, and ~40-50% equity vs her betting range (beats bluffs/draws, behind Jx/sets). Pot odds are excellent: 200 to call into 800 (~4:1, 20% equity needed, we have much more). OOP, calling keeps pot small, allows control; we can check-fold turn to aggression if board worsens or check-call/raise if improves. Raising is tempting for value/protection but thin OOP with vulnerable pair on wet board—risks folding out better equity realizations or facing tough river spots. Folding wastes massive EV with this price and hand strength. Call maximizes EV, realizes equity cheaply.

Turn pairs my Q: I hold Qh7c on Jc-4s-5c-Qs. Pot is 800 and Elon (BB) called my flop c-bet, so his range is wide (Jx, small pairs, club draws, Qx, A-highs). I now have top pair with a weak kicker — want to get value from worse pairs and charge draws while keeping the pot controllable with deep stacks. A half-pot value bet (~400) achieves that: it gets called by Jx/5x/4x and many worse Qx while denying free cards to club/overcard draws. If he raises big I’ll fold (kicker is vulnerable); if called I’ll re-evaluate on the river and proceed with pot control or a small value line depending on the runout.

On the turn, the Qs arrives, adding a spade to the board (now two spades, still low) but primarily improving Minni's range by hitting Qx hands, completing some straight draws (e.g., KT for QJT straight, or other broadways), and not helping us at all. We hold bottom pair with a K kicker (4h pairing 4s), but this is highly vulnerable OOP: loses to all Jx, Qx, 55 sets, higher pairs, two-pair combos, and any completed draws. Minni's line—small SB open, small flop c-bet on draw-heavy board, continued turn bet sizing up slightly—polarizes her range toward Jx value, stubborn draws/clubs that picked up equity, or bluffs that continue. However, her aggression indicates strength more often than not, especially after we called flop. Pot odds are 400 into 1200 (~25%, needing ~20% equity), but our equity vs. her betting range drops below 30% (beats pure bluffs/missed clubs but crushed by value). River OOP with one card left, no reliable draw, and deep stacks make check-calling or floating -EV; raising is a bluff with poor blockers and no fold equity on this board. Folding realizes equity preflop/flop without risking further downside on a deteriorating spot, preserving stack for better leverage later. Long-term EV positive to fold marginal made hands to aggression on scary turns.

Three-handed on the button QJo is a strong opening hand — lots of high-card and straight potential plus full positional advantage. With deep stacks (100+ BB) and both opponents capable of folding, I want to take the initiative and get fold equity rather than limp. A standard 3x BB open is a balanced size: big enough to discourage loose callers and isolate, but small enough to keep postflop play manageable vs a 3-bet. Plan: c-bet favorable flops and use position to apply pressure; fold to a large, committed 3-bet from Elon but defend/call smaller 3-bets depending on odds and opponent tendencies.

AdJd is a premium suited Broadway hand from the small blind facing a button min-raise. It has excellent equity against Minni's likely wide button range, dominates many of her calling hands, and blocks strong Ax hands. 3-betting builds the pot for value, applies fold equity on Minni (who opens wide but folds to aggression often per history), and denies BB equity. Standard sizing around 3x the raise OOP. If called, we have postflop playability with flush draw potential and high cards. Calling is too passive OOP; folding is -EV with this strength. Deep stacks allow for flexible play postflop.

I am in the Big Blind with Ten-Six offsuit. The Button (Minni) opened and the Small Blind (Elon) 3-bet to 900. My hand is very weak and I will be out of position against two active and aggressive players. There is no strategic value in calling or 4-betting here. I will fold and wait for a better spot to utilize my stack.

Elon 3-bet to 900 over my 300 button open. I have Q♠J♣ — a hand with good equity and excellent postflop playability in position three-handed. The extra 600 to call is a reasonable price versus his likely 3‑bet range (broadways + some value/air), and stacks are deep enough to realize equity and use position. 4‑betting as a bluff risks a costly 5‑bet or getting called by better hands; folding is too tight. Plan: call to see a flop, use positional advantage to c-bet favorable textures, float and apply pressure when appropriate, and fold to heavy aggression on clearly dominated boards.

Preflop 3-bet with AdJd was standard OOP vs button open, getting value and position denial. Flop 7d5h8s gives me the nut diamond flush draw (9 outs, ~35% equity if called), two live overcards (A,J block strong hands), and backdoor straight potential. OOP first to act vs Minni's 3-bet calling button range (pairs 66+, AQs+, suited connectors 65s-98s, etc.). Many of her hands miss (overpairs vulnerable, Ax drawing thin), so strong semi-bluff spot: c-bet for value from missed Ax/draws/worse pairs, protection, fold equity (she folds to aggression often per history e.g. hands 84,93), and build pot OOP with equity. Sizing 1200 (~65% pot) balances value/protection, denies equity, keeps stacks playable (~8bb committed). Check too passive, risks giving free cards/multiway if Flash wakes up (though folded pre). Check-raise unnecessary OOP.

Elon 3‑bet preflop and is making a small continuation bet (1200 into a 3100 pot). I have Q♠J♣ — two clean overcards and excellent post‑flop position. Calling 1200 into a pot that will be 5500 if I call means I need ~21.8% equity (1200/5500). My hand has ~24%+ raw chance to pair by the river and additional equity versus missed c‑bets in his range, so I expect my equity vs his c‑bet range to be comfortably above the required threshold. Raising to the min (2400) here is thin: I lack the fold equity and could face a shove or sticky calls from better hands, unnecessarily inflating a pot I can play well in position. Plan: call, keep pot controllable, and use position to float barreled turns, extract value on Q/J/broadway turns, or fold to heavy turn aggression when I miss. Call 1200 and reassess on the turn.

Turn 8c pairs the board, creating a paired texture that favors my range as the preflop 3-bettor (includes more 8x, sets, overpairs). I hold the nut diamond flush draw (9 outs ~18% immediate, more with overcards/backdoors), live A/J blockers reducing her strong Ax pairs. Minni's flop call from button vs 3bet range: overpairs QQ+, 88-99, draws (67s,96s, etc.), Ax suited. Paired 8 hits some (89,T8,98), but many miss/improve weakly; she floats/turns pairs light per history (calls multiple streets with medium strength but folds to aggression e.g. hands 25,21,30). OOP with strong equity, betting extracts value from worse pairs/draws (77-66, lower 8x, incomplete draws), protects vs river diamonds hitting her, applies fold equity to overpairs wary of 98s/sets. ~70% pot size (3000 into 4300) balances value/protection, denies equity/shrinks her calling range, leaves stack playable (~5800 behind for river shove if called). Check too passive: gives free card to overpairs/draws, risks multiway if Flash re-enters (unlikely), loses value. No fold with 35%+ equity + fold equity. Sizing avoids pot commitment pre-river flexibility.

Elon was the preflop 3‑better and has continued with a c‑bet on the flop and now a large turn bet. I hold Q♠J♣ on 7♦‑5♥‑8♠‑8♣ — after the turn I have no pair and my only clean outs are three remaining Queens and three remaining Jacks (6 outs ≈ 13% to hit the river). The math is clear: calling 3,000 into a 7,300 pot requires roughly 3,000/(7,300+3,000) ≈ 29% equity to be break‑even. My ~13% raw chance to improve is well below that. Raising as a bluff is thin — I have poor blockers, no showdown value, and raising would either commit a lot of chips or turn this into a fold/call-versus-all‑in situation against a very strong line. With deep stacks but only three players left, preserving chips and waiting for a better spot is the right play. Fold.

On the button with 6d8h offsuit, a speculative connected hand that has potential to flop strong draws or straights. With 3 players left, deep stacks (~130BB effective), and position on both opponents, this is well within a standard button opening range (40-60% of hands). Raising takes initiative, has fold equity against blinds, and allows value if called due to playability post-flop. Limping is too passive and invites multiway pots without initiative. Folding wastes button value in a tournament spot where stealing blinds/chips is key to building stack. Standard raise size 3x BB to 300, consistent with observed opening sizes in prior hands.

I am holding 7d 4s in the small blind, which is a very weak hand. Elon has raised from the button, and playing this hand out of position would be highly unprofitable in the long run. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I’m in the big blind with 2s 9h (deuce of spades, nine of hearts). Elon open-raised to 300 from the button; I must call 200 into a 450 pot. Calling requires ~200/(450+200) = 30.8% equity to break even. 2-9 offsuit is one of the weakest hands preflop — heavily dominated by a standard button opening range and has very poor postflop playability and blocker value. A minimum three-bet bluff to 500 is possible in theory but with this hand I don’t want to face further aggression out of position or risk bloating the pot with very little equity. Preserving my 14k+ stack for better spots and folding here is the +EV play.

I am on the button in a 3-handed game holding A2o. While Ace-Deuce is a relatively weak Ace, it is a standard opening hand from the button in a short-handed environment to pick up the blinds or play in position. My standard opening raise throughout this tournament has been 300 chips, and I will maintain that consistency here.

9♠7♥ is a weak, offsuit hand with poor post‑flop playability and I’m out of position to the button (and possibly the BB). Flash opened to 300 (3x) — calling the extra 250 to defend will often leave me OOP in a multiway pot with dominated or marginal equity, and a min‑raise to 500 is just a thin bluff with little fold equity or meaningful blockers. With healthy stacks I should conserve chips and wait for a better spot in position. Fold.

Facing a button raise to 300 in the big blind with Th8c offsuit, a marginal hand with poor playability and low equity against a wide button opening range. Pot odds are about 2.25:1 for calling 200 more, but postflop I'll be out of position against an aggressive opponent like Flash who frequently raises from late position. Folding preserves stack for better spots; no strong blockers for a 3-bet bluff, and defending this wide isn't +EV long-term in a tournament setting with deep stacks.

On the button with 3♥4♥ and deep stacks (~140 BB) in a 3-handed game this is a playable, speculative hand with great post‑flop implied odds and positional advantage. Facing only the blinds, I want fold equity and initiative — calling 100 is passive and often gives cheap multiway pots; folding is too tight. I open to 300 (3×BB) — a standard, credible size that pressures the blinds and preserves postflop maneuverability. Plan: fold to a large 3‑bet, but if called use position to c‑bet favorable textures, realize equity on draws, and apply pressure when appropriate.

Preflop in SB with 3c4c suited connectors. Minni raises to 300 from BTN, standard 3x raise. Pot is 450, need to call 250 more (already in for SB 50), offering about 2.8:1 odds. 34s has good playability deepstacked (130BB+), potential for strong draws multiway if BB calls. BTN range wide, equity decent (~30-35% vs loose open). OOP but speculative hands defend wide vs BTN. Folding too tight; 3bet not suitable for this hand. Call to realize equity and implied odds.

I am in the Big Blind with Queen-Deuce offsuit. Minni opened from the button for 3x and Elon called from the Small Blind. While the pot odds are 4.5:1 to call, Q2o is a very weak hand to play multi-way and out of position against two active opponents. Given the tournament stage and the lack of playability for this hand, folding is the most disciplined choice to preserve my stack.

Flop of 7c-2d-Jc gives me a strong nut flush draw with 4c-3c (9 clean outs, ~18% equity by turn, ~35% to river), on a draw-heavy board (two clubs, possible straight draws like QT, KT, 87s). Preflop called OOP with suited connectors for playability, pot now 700, deep stacks (~120BB). As preflop caller first to act, checking represents strength selectively while disguising the draw, induces cbets from button's wide range (Ax suited, broadway, pairs), sets up check-raise semi-bluff for fold equity/value, or check-call if check-back. Betting risks check-raise from stronger hands/draws, lower EV OOP. Balance range with pure air/checks, monsters check too. Maximize equity realization and fold equity post-turn if needed.

I opened from the button and Elon (SB) just called preflop. Flop Jc-7c-2d is very dry for my 3♥4♥ — I have no pair and no meaningful draw, but the board misses a large portion of SB’s calling range (broadways, many pocket pairs and weak Ax). Elon checked, so a standard continuation bet can pick up the pot now or charge his weaker hands and draws. I’ll make a half‑pot c-bet (350) to apply pressure while keeping the sizing controllable with deep stacks. Plan: take it down if he folds; if he calls, re-evaluate on the turn and give up to a significant raise — I have very little showdown equity vs a raise or a show of strength.

Facing a 1/2 pot cbet on a draw-heavy flop (7c2dJc) with nut flush draw (9 outs, ~35% equity). Deep stacks (~120BB). OOP check-raise semi-bluff targets button's wide polarized cbet range (Ax suited, broadway, small pairs, bluffs) for fold equity while retaining strong equity if called. Sizing to ~3x her bet (1050 total) builds pot, denies equity, sets up turn flexibility. Superior to check-call (OOP multiway risk if she checks back) or fold (missing value). Consistent with preflop plan to realize equity aggressively.

Elon check‑raised my small c‑bet from 350 up to 1050. The pot is 2100 and it’s 700 to call, so I need roughly 25% equity to justify a call. With 3♥4♥ on Jc‑7c‑2d I have no pair and essentially no meaningful draws (a flush is impossible — I’d need three hearts on board but only two cards remain; the chance to pair a 3 or 4 by river is only ~12–13%). Elon’s check‑raise is weighted toward value (Jx, pocket pairs, 7x, sets) and while he may bluff sometimes, my raw equity is well below the break‑even point. Raising would be an unanchored big bluff with poor fold equity and unnecessary risk given deep stacks. Fold and wait for a better, higher‑equity spot in position.

3-handed on the button with T5s, a suited connector with good playability and position. Standard to raise preflop to steal the dead money from blinds or play postflop in position. Raise to 3x BB (300 total) for value and isolation. Pot odds poor for flat if they defend wide, but raising builds the pot advantageously.

I am holding pocket sevens in the Small Blind. This is a strong starting hand, but playing it out of position against a button raise by calling is generally sub-optimal. To take control of the hand, define Elon's range, and potentially win the pot immediately, a 3-bet is the standard professional play. A raise to 1000 (roughly 3.3x the open) is a balanced sizing that puts pressure on the button and discourages the Big Blind from entering light.

Elon opened to 300 and Flash (SB) 3-bet to 1000. Pot is 1400 and it costs me 900 more to call (total 1000 committed). Calling 900 to win 2300 requires ~39% equity. Q♦5♠ is a weak, often dominated hand in a 3-way pot and plays poorly out of position postflop. Flash’s cold 3-bet from the SB polarizes/represents a reasonably strong range, and Elon still in the hand makes this a tough multiway spot. A 4-bet bluff here is thin and likely to get called or put me in a bloated pot out of position. With a healthy stack and many hands left, preserving chips and folding is the +EV play.

3-handed on button facing SB 3-bet after my open-raise. T5s is a suited connector with excellent playability, position, and equity against a likely polarized SB 3-bet range (value like AQ+, sets, plus bluffs). Stacks are deep (~100BB effective), providing strong implied odds if we hit two-pair, straight, flush draw, etc. Pot odds are favorable: calling 700 into 1400 (~2:1 direct, better implied). Folding is too tight given position and hand strength; 4-betting risks bloating pot OOP for opponent with marginal blocker equity. Calling keeps pot manageable, realizes equity postflop in position, and allows flexible play (e.g., check-raise flops, float turns). Consistent with aggressive preflop strategy while balancing.

On a flop of King-Queen-Ten, my pocket sevens are in a very vulnerable position. This is a highly coordinated board that heavily favors the caller's range (Elon), as it hits hands like K-Q, Q-T, J-T, A-J, and A-Q—all of which are likely to call a 3-bet. As the pre-flop aggressor out of position, checking is the most prudent move. I have very little equity against Elon's continuing range, and a continuation bet is unlikely to fold out any hand that connected with this board. I will check and likely fold to any significant aggression.

Flop board Qs Kd Th is highly coordinated with straight draws (e.g., J9, AJo), wheel draws, and overpair potential. Holding Tc5c gives top pair (tens) with a weak 5 kicker and suited backdoor flush draw. Called a SB 3-bet preflop with T5s for playability, position, and equity. Flash's check after 3-bet suggests possible missed value (AK, AQ), air, or trapping with monster, but SB often checks strong hands too. In position on button vs single opponent, betting protects against draws (e.g., J9s, QJs), denies equity, builds pot when likely ahead vs bluffs/weak pairs, and charges implied odds. Checking risks free card to improve opponent or peel off equity. Bet 2/3 pot (1400 into 2100) balances value/protection without overbetting OOP check. Deep stacks allow fold equity and postflop flexibility if raised.

On this highly coordinated board of Ks-Qs-Th, my pocket sevens are extremely vulnerable. This texture hits almost the entirety of Elon's flatting range for a 3-bet (Broadway cards, suited connectors, and medium-to-high pairs). There is almost no hand Elon would bet for 1400 that sevens are beating, and I have very little equity to improve to a winning hand on the turn or river. Continuing out of position against such heavy board interaction would be a massive mistake. I will fold.

I am on the button with Ace-Eight offsuit, which is a strong starting hand in a three-handed game. Standard professional play dictates an open-raise here to take control of the pot and potentially win the blinds immediately. I will raise to 300, which is consistent with the standard opening size seen throughout this tournament.

Fold. 7♦4♥ is a very weak, frequently dominated hand and I’m out of position against the Button’s open with the Big Blind still to act. Pot is 450 and I must call 250 (to 300) — that gives me ~26% required equity to justify a call, which 7-4o rarely has vs a Button raising range (and it’s a multiway/OO P situation if Elon defends). A 3-bet bluff to 500 is too thin here given stack depths and dynamics. Preserve a healthy 135+ BB stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

Preflop in BB facing a button raise to 300 after SB folds. Pot is 450, calling 200 more for total commitment of 300. 9dTh is a suited connector with good playability, straight and flush potential. Button ranges are wide (steal-heavy), so equity is favorable (~35-40% vs wide range). Deep stacks (149BB effective) provide implied odds. OOP but prefer calling over folding trash BB defense or 3-betting a marginal hand. Plan to play post-flop aggressively if hit, fold to c-bet if miss. Maximizes EV long-term.

Flop completely misses my 9d Th: no pair, no straight draw (gutshot unlikely), no flush draw. Board is paired 4s with a 5, fairly dry and low, favoring villain's wide button range for continuation betting but also giving them showdown value often. OOP first to act with zero equity/showdown, standard check-fold to any reasonable bet size. No donk value or semi-bluff here. Preserves stack for better spots in late tournament with deep stacks.

On a dry, paired flop of 4-4-5, I have the range advantage as the pre-flop aggressor. Elon checked, which is expected. I will place a continuation bet of 300 chips (approximately 46% of the pot) to take down the hand now and fold out his non-pairing overcards and suited connectors. Ace-high often holds the best hand here, but betting protects my equity and maintains the initiative.

Flop completely misses my T9 suited: no pair, no straight draw (not even a gutshot; board 4h4s5d pairs low cards without connecting to my holdings), no flush draw. Pot 950, facing 300 bet (1/3 pot) from button who raised preflop after SB fold – their range is wide (Ax, Kx, suited connectors, pairs, bluffs) and they c-bet frequently on this dry paired board for value/thin value/protection/fold equity. OOP with zero equity or fold equity, calling commits to further tough decisions on turn/river without improving often enough. Pot odds ~3:1 but equity <20% vs c-bet range, negative EV. Folding preserves deep stack (14690, ~147BB) for premium spots in late stages (hand 125/150, 3 players left). Consistent with preflop plan to fold misses.

I’m on the button with T9o (9c Ts), deep stacks (~136 BB) and only the blinds to act. This is a clear open-raising spot — T9o plays well in position and I want to steal the blinds or play the pot in position. The min-raise is 200 but the table’s standard open size has been ~300; I’ll raise to 300 (3x BB) to apply pressure on the blinds and keep my range balanced. Plan: play postflop in position — c-bet favorable textures, control the pot on wides, and fold to large / polarizing 3-bets (I’ll re-evaluate small 3-bets depending on size and opponent).

Pocket fours is a strong small pair with set-mining potential and good playability. Facing a button raise to 300, which is likely a wide range given Minni's aggressive history from late position. In the small blind, calling offers implied odds with deep stacks (pot 450, calling 250 more for ~26% pot commitment initially), but being OOP post-flop against the button is tough, especially multiway if BB calls. A 3-bet isolates the button, denies equity to worse hands, and builds the pot with our premium hand. Standard sizing is about 3x the raise (900 total), which is profitable against a wide calling range while keeping pot manageable. Flash in BB folds often to 3-bets. This maximizes EV long-term in late tournament with 24 hands left.

I am holding pocket Queens, which is a premium hand. Minni raised from the button and Elon 3-bet from the small blind. While this action shows strength, QQ is clearly ahead of a wide 3-betting range in this spot. I will 4-bet to 2400 to take initiative, define my hand strength, and potentially end the hand now or build a pot where I am likely a favorite.

I opened to 300 on the button with T9o and got a 3‑bet from SB to 900 and a 4‑bet from BB to 2400. Pot is 3600 and it would cost me 2100 to call. This is a classic multiway 4‑bet spot where ranges are heavily value‑weighted (TT+/AK, polar 4‑bets) and my hand has poor equity and many dominated combos. Calling for ~2.1k (≈37% immediate pot odds) is not profitable vs those ranges and would leave me OOP and playing a large pot against two players. A 5‑bet shove as a bluff is too risky here with deep stacks and little blocker value. Preserve the stack and wait for a better spot in position — fold.

Pocket fours faced a BB squeeze 4-bet to 2400 after my isolation 3-bet over the button open. Flash's range is strong: QQ+, AK, possibly JJ-TT or bluffs, but given history of aggression, likely premium. 44 has ~18-22% equity vs that range heads-up, but OOP post-flop play is difficult against a polarized continuing range. Direct pot odds require ~23% equity (1500 to call into ~5100 pot), marginal even with set-mining implied odds (~12:1 needed, but realization low). 5-bet jamming 13790 risks stack vs better pairs/monsters; calling bloats pot OOP. Standard fold preserves stack for superior spots late in tournament (24 hands left).

KK is the second-best starting hand in poker, especially strong on the button with position on both blinds. Deep stacks allow for aggressive play to build the pot while denying equity to worse hands. Standard raise size here is about 3-4x the big blind (300-400). Opponents have shown they call raises with wide ranges in position, but raising isolates and takes advantage of fold equity. Raising to 400 total builds the pot without overcommitting.

I am in the Small Blind with J♥7♦. Elon has opened from the Button with a 4x raise (400 chips). In a 3-handed game, while ranges are wider, J7o is still a weak hand to defend out of position against a large open. Given the stack sizes are deep and the current tournament stage, I prefer folding and waiting for a more profitable spot rather than playing a marginal hand out of position.

Elon opened to 400 from the button in a 3‑handed pot and the SB folded. I have A♠6♥ (ace with a weak kicker) in the big blind. This is a hand I want to defend vs a wide button opening — it has good showdown potential (top pair chances) and blocks some A‑x combos, but it’s not great for a 3‑bet because it’s easily dominated by better Aces and playing a bloated pot out of position is costly. I’ll call the 300 more (400 total) to see a flop and play post‑flop based on texture: if I hit an Ace I can value/raise cautiously, if I pick up strong draws I’ll continue, otherwise I’ll control the pot and fold to heavy aggression.

I missed this dry, paired flop with A6. The board (7♥ 3♠ 3♣) gives me no straight/flush draws and I only have Ace‑high — I called preflop to defend vs a wide button range, but I don’t want to inflate a bloated 3‑handed pot out of position with a marginal hand. Check for pot control and information: if Elon barrels a small C‑bet I can call to realize my showdown equity and potentially improve to top pair on the turn; if he fires a large, polarized bet I’ll fold (he’s representing trips/7x/strong Ax/overpairs). If I pair my Ace on the turn I’ll start extracting value.

KK is an overpair to a paired, dry flop (7h-3s-3c). Minni checked from the BB after calling my preflop raise, representing a wide calling range that KK crushes: weaker pairs, Ax high, suited connectors without improvement. This board has little draw potential (no flush draw complete, straight draws unlikely), so betting extracts value from worse hands while protecting against overcards or runner-runner draws. Pot is 850; a 2/3 pot bet of 600 builds the pot without overexposing to floats or traps, maintaining aggression with position. Checking would give free cards to incomplete hands and miss value.

Flop is 7♥ 3♠ 3♣; I have A♥6♠. Elon opened wide from the button preflop and now makes a medium c‑bet of 600 into a 1450 pot. This is exactly the sort of spot I wanted to defend preflop — my Ace has decent standalone showdown value, I block some A‑x value combos, and the bet size gives me reasonable pot odds (~600 to win ~2050 ⇒ ~29% equity required). Raising here with only Ace‑high would be too risky (I’d be polarizing into sets/trips/7x/strong overpairs that will call or shove), while folding is too weak given my equity and the small relative cost. I’ll call 600 to control the pot, realize equity and see a turn — if I hit an Ace I can extract value, otherwise I can fold to big turn aggression representing trips/strong made hands.

I improved to top pair on the turn (A♣ pairs my A♥) after defending preflop and calling the flop — this is exactly the spot I wanted to extract value. Elon opened wide preflop and barreled the flop, so his continuing range contains a fair number of worse A‑x hands and medium pairs (and some stronger 3x/full‑house combos). Betting here gets value from those worse Aces and medium made hands and denies a free river card to any curious turn‑cards; it also keeps the pot controlled vs the small subset of hands that beat me. Pot = 2,050 so a roughly half‑pot bet (~1,000) is large enough to get calls from worse A‑x and K/Qx while still allowing me to fold to a sizable check‑raise (which would usually represent trips/full house). I’ll bet 1,000 and re‑evaluate based on Elon’s response — call small/medium action, fold to a big raise.

KK remains a premium hand post-turn despite the Ac, as it blocks AA and crushes weaker pairs, broadway that missed, and many floats. Minni's OOP donk-bet sizing (1000 into 3050, ~1/3 pot) after calling flop c-bet is polarized: strong Ax (A7s, A3s, weak Ax), sets (77), or bluffs/draws turning aggression. Her history shows wide calling ranges pre/flop but folding to pressure later (e.g., Hands 2,3,4,14,15). Position allows controlling pot; calling risks free river cards to clubs/flush draws or runner-runners, and pot odds (4:1) don't justify if often behind Ax. Raising builds pot for value from worse Ax/weak pairs while folding out bluffs/draws. Size to ~pot (3000 total: call 1000 + raise 2000, 3x her bet) balances fold equity/value extraction without overcommitting deep stacks (~12k effective). Folds monsters prepping shoves, but rare OOP.

I open‑raised preflop and called the flop to realize equity; I bet the turn (1,000) after pairing my Ace to extract value from worse A‑x and medium pairs. Elon’s large check‑raise to 3,000 (I must call 2,000 into a 6,050 pot) is highly polarizing — he’s representing sets/full houses or A‑x with better kickers, with only a thin bluffing frequency. Calling requires ~25% equity (2,000 to win ~8,050) but against his line and sizing my actual equity is likely lower and I’m dominated by many of his value combos; reraising would commit too many chips and risk a shove. I’ll fold and preserve my stack for better spots.

Holding Ace-Jack offsuit on the button in a three-handed game is a premium situation. This hand is well ahead of the average range of both the small blind and the big blind. I will put in a standard raise to 300 to take control of the pot and build value, while also providing the opportunity to win the blinds and antes immediately. Based on the previous hands, both Minni and Elon are active, so I expect a call or a potential 3-bet, but AJ is strong enough to play against most of their ranges.

I have T♠2♦ in the small blind facing a button open to 3bb (to call 250). This is a very weak, unplayable hand out of position — it will be dominated by typical opener ranges (A/K/Tx, broadways, suited connectors) and has poor equity. Calling 250 to play OOP is a losing proposition long-term (break-even equity ~26% vs the pot odds), and a light 3-bet to 500 with this holding is a bad bluff candidate (no meaningful blockers, big blind can still defend or 4-bet). My stack is healthy (~112bb) with 22 hands left, so preserve chips and wait for a better spot. Fold.

74d is a suited connector with good playability, flush and straight potential. In BB vs BTN raise, Flash opens wide from button as seen in numerous previous hands (e.g., #126, #125, #124, etc.). Deep stacks (157bb effective) provide excellent implied odds for set-mining draws OOP. Pot odds: calling 200 more into 450 (pot becomes 650, ~3.25:1). Folding is too tight in this spot; 3-betting not suitable for this hand. Calling to realize equity and play postflop.

Flop of 3s5s2s is extremely wet: three spades, connected low cards enabling wheel straight draws (A6, 46, 64 best) and flush draws. My 7d4d has a weak gutshot straight draw to the 6 (for 2-3-4-5-6 wheel) and backdoor diamond flush draw, but no spade so missing the primary draw. OOP as BB vs Button raiser (Flash, who opens wide frequently from history). Deep stacks (~155bb effective), but this hand has poor equity (~20-25% vs typical button calling range including Ax suited spade, broadway spade, pairs). Pot odds irrelevant since acting first. Donk betting risks folding out better/worse while getting raised off equity; standard check to pot control, realize any showdown value if Flash checks behind weak hands, or fold to bet. Bluff-raising too thin OOP on this texture where Flash can have nutted draws/sets easily. Checking optimal for balancing range and avoiding tough spots postflop.

On a monotone board of 3s-5s-2s, I hold the Ace of Spades, giving me the nut flush draw and the nut blocker. Elon checked to me, which often signals weakness on such a coordinated board. I will place a continuation bet of 350 (just over half the pot) to exert pressure. This serves as a semi-bluff; if Elon folds, I win the pot immediately. If he calls, I still have significant equity to improve to the nut flush or potentially top pair with my overcards.

Facing a 350 bet into 1000 pot on a extremely wet flop (3s5s2s) with 74dd, which has only a weak gutshot to the 6 (for 23456 straight) and backdoor diamond draw, but no spade. Equity ~20% vs Flash's button range (which includes many strong spade flush draws, straight draws like A4s/64s, sets), barely meeting pot odds (350/1350 ~26% pot, but effective equity lower due to reverse implied). OOP deepstacked, calling commits to tough turn spots where Flash can continue with nutted hands/draws or check behind weak air I beat. Bluff-raising thin OOP risks getting snapped by better draws/sets and folding out bluffs. Folding preserves stack for better spots; pot control achieved by check, now pot control by fold to avoid realization issues.

Button with A♦Q♠ in a three‑handed pot is a strong, late‑position raising spot. Blinds are 50/100 (pot 150) and the min‑raise is 200, but I want a standard open size to take initiative and extract value: raise to 3x the BB (300 total). This isolates the blinds, gives fold equity, and keeps post‑flop decisions comfortable with deep stacks (my 11,220 vs Elon 15,490 and Flash 13,140). Plan: c‑bet a wide range on dry flops, control pot size on wet/dangerous boards, and re‑evaluate if I face a large 3‑bet (fold to extreme aggression, call or 4‑bet selectively based on sizing and opponent).

Preflop in SB facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB). My hand Ah9d is a strong defender OOP vs a wide button opening range. A9o blocks AA, AK, AQ and has good playability with ace-high, gutshots, and overcard potential. Pot odds are favorable (pot 450 to call 250 more, total pot ~700 after call). Folding is too tight given Minni's frequent late-position opens from history; 3-betting risks bloating pot OOP with a marginal ace. Calling keeps pot multiway potential with BB, allows realizing equity postflop, and preserves stack in late tournament (hand 129/150, deep effective stacks). Will reassess on flop.

I am in the Big Blind with Ten-Three offsuit. Minni raised from the button and Elon called from the Small Blind. Although the pot odds are approximately 4.5-to-1 (calling 200 to potentially win a 900 pot), T3o is an extremely weak hand with poor playability, especially when out of position against two opponents. I have a healthy stack and see little value in defending with a hand that is likely dominated and difficult to play post-flop.

Flop Ac Tc 7h gives me top pair (Ah) with a weak 9 kicker on a coordinated board with club flush draw, open-ended straight draws (JQ, KQ), and possible two-pair/set hands. As first to act OOP after calling a button raise, checking invites bluffs but risks giving free cards to draws or allowing Minni to rep strength. Minni's button range includes Ax (weaker kickers blocked somewhat), Tx, 87s, suited connectors, which hit this board decently. Betting protects against draws, gets value from worse Ax/Tx pairs, and denies equity. Pot 700, bet sizing 1/2 pot (350-400) balances value/protection without overbetting into monsters. Deep stacks (15k eff vs Minni 10k) support continuation. Will c/f or c/c depending on response.

I opened to 300 from the button and Elon called. Flop A♣ T♣ 7♥ I hold A♦Q♠ — top pair with a good kicker. Elon leads 400 into the ~700 pre‑bet pot (pot is 1100), a medium c‑bet that can be a wide range (worse aces, middle pairs, club draws, bluffs). With position and deep stacks I want to keep worse hands and draws in while avoiding bloating the pot and facing a big check‑raise or turn shove. I’ll call the 400 to control the pot size, realize equity, and re‑evaluate on the turn (bet for value on safe cards, proceed cautiously if a club or heavy aggression appears).

Turn 5s adds a spade flush draw to the board but doesn't complete the club flush draw or major straight draws (possible gutshots like 68 or 89 remain live but unimproved). My Ah9d holds top pair with a weak 9 kicker on a pot of 1500, OOP after Minni called my flop c-bet. Her calling range includes weaker Ax (A8-AJ, suited), Tx, sets, two-pairs (AT, A7, T7), and stubborn draws. Continued betting risks folding out worse hands while getting raised by better Ax/Tx/sets; pot control is key with marginal strength and deep stacks (eff ~10k). Checking induces bluffs from missed draws, allows cheap realization of equity on river, and avoids bloating pot OOP late in tournament. Will evaluate Minni's response: c/c thinner value, c/f to aggression, fold to huge overbets.

I have A♦Q♠ on A♣ T♣ 7♥ 5♠ — top pair with a very good kicker. Elon checked the turn after barreling the flop, which looks like weakness or pot‑control; his range includes worse aces, tens, medium pairs and club draws. I want to extract thin value from worse A/x, T/x and charge club draws while keeping the pot manageable. A half‑pot bet (~750 into the 1500 pot) accomplishes that: it’s big enough to deny free cards to draws and get called by worse hands, but not so large that I fold out many value calls or overcommit if I face a big check‑raise. If called I’ll re‑evaluate on the river; if Elon check‑raises big I’ll give credit to sets/two‑pair and proceed cautiously based on sizing.

Turn board 5s is a low, somewhat blank card that doesn't drastically change the board dynamics—it adds a spade flush draw possibility but misses most club flush draws and doesn't complete major straight draws (e.g., J8 or 89 now have gutshots at best). I hold top pair Ah9d with a weak kicker on a pot of 1500 pre-Minni's bet. After my flop c-bet call from her, her range for calling includes weaker Ax (A8-AJ, suited), Tx pairs/combos, two-pair combos (A7, T7, A10 suited), sets (77, TT, AA unlikely), and semi-bluffs like flush/straight draws. Her 750 bet (~1/2 pot) after my check can be value from better hands or thin value/protection from medium strength; bluffs from missed draws are possible given her aggressive history (frequent button opens and postflop aggression seen in hands like #25, #21, #109). Pot odds are excellent: calling 750 into 2250 (becomes 3000 pot) requires ~25% equity, which I have vs her range (beats worse Ax/Tx ~30-40% of value range, plus showdown vs draws). Folding top pair OOP to 1/2 pot is too tight late in tournament with deep effective stacks (~9.7k with Minni); calling realizes equity, controls pot size OOP, and sets up river decisions: c/c thin value/implied from worse, c/f to further aggression or river leads. Raising risks isolation OOP vs better hands/draws with poor post-oak equity. Consistent with pre-turn plan to c/c thinner value here.

River Qd is a mixed card: completes broadway straights for KJ (in her calling range from button raise/flop c-call/turn call), pairs the board possibly for Tx two-pair, but misses club flushes and most straight draws (e.g., J8 gutshot missed, 89 gutshot). My Ah9d remains top pair weak kicker, beating worse Ax (A8/AJ suited/blockers), Tx pairs, missed draws, bluffs; loses to AJ+/AQ, AT combos, sets, two-pairs, straights. Heads-up OOP first to act into pot of 3000 with ~14k stack vs Minni's 9.7k eff. Her range after calling flop 400 c-bet (on wet board), my turn check-call her 750 (delayed c-bet range: value Ax/Tx/sets + draws/protection): polarized to strong value/stubborn medium + missed semi-bluffs. Betting thin value risks fold equity from worse or raise/fold by better; poor with marginal hand/deep stacks. Checking maximizes EV: pot control OOP, induces bluffs/checks from missed draws (club/spade/SD she rep'd flop), cheap showdown vs medium strength, ~33-50% equity vs her range. History shows Minni aggressive (e.g., #25 multi-street value, #50 river bet/call stations, #109 river bluff-catch by me) but exploitable calling station OOP. Will c/call 1/2-2/3 pot (~1500-2000), c/f larger/overbets/raises, consistent with turn plan to c/c thin, realizes equity late tournament (hand 129/150, ICM pressure low with 3 left). Fold impossible (0 to call), raise pointless (no bet). Check optimal.

I hold A♦Q♠ and the river Q♦ gives me two pair, Aces and Queens — a very strong showdown hand. Elon’s line (pre‑flop call, flop lead of 400, then checking the turn and calling my 750) is consistent with a range of worse A‑x, T‑x, medium pairs and a few pocket pairs/sets. Only sets (77, TT) or a turned set (55) realistically beat me. Most of his calling range is behind and will call a value bet. With the pot at 3,000 I want to extract value without bloating the pot or folding out the worse hands. A half‑pot bet (~1,500) is the right sizing — it gets called by worse A’s, tens and medium pairs, denies missed draws a free card and leaves room to fold to a large check‑raise (which would represent a set/full house). Bet 1,500.

River Qd completes some straight draws like KJ (broadway), which is plausibly in Minni's button raising/calling range (KJ suited clubs fits flop c-bet call on AcTc7h board), and improves some Qx hands, but misses club flushes, spade flushes unimproved, and many gutshots. However, it doesn't change core dynamics much: board still value-heavy for Ax/Tx. Heads-up OOP on river facing 1500 bet (~1/3 pot into 4500) after pot-controlled check; pot odds ~3:1 require ~25% equity. Ah9d (TPWK) beats her weaker Ax (A8-AT suited, ~20-30% range), Tx pairs (~15%), missed draws/bluffs (~20-30%, given her aggression in hands like #25 multi-street value into calls, #109 river thin value, #102 river calls); loses to AJ+/sets/two-pair/straights (~40%). Rough equity ~35-45% vs polarized value+bluff range (GTO sims often call TP9+ here). Folding TP OOP to 1/3 pot too tight late (hand 129/150, ICM mild with 3 deep stacks, my 14k covers call easily, eff 8k with her). Raising polarizes poorly (weak kicker, reverse implied OOP). Calling realizes equity, controls final pot OOP, exploits her history of thin value/overvaluing medium strength (e.g., #50 river bet called). Consistent with turn/river plan to c/c thin value.

3-handed on the button with deep stacks (126BB effective). Held 5d3h is a trash hand: offsuit, low cards, disconnected. Even in 3-handed play where button opens very wide (~50%+), 53o is below the opening range due to poor playability postflop and low equity against calling ranges. Limping invites multiway pots where this hand performs poorly. Raising risks playing out of position postflop with a weak hand. Best to fold and wait for better spots this late in the tournament (hand 130/150) to maximize stack.

I'm in the Small Blind with a suited Queen. Elon has folded on the Button, so it's just me and Minni in the Big Blind. Minni has been folding to a fair number of late-position steals recently. Q2 suited is a hand that plays well enough post-flop if called, but the main objective here is to take the blinds. I will put in a standard raise to 300 to pressure the Big Blind.

I’m in the big blind with 9♦8♠ facing a standard SB open to 300. I already have 100 posted and need 200 more to call (total commit 300). 9‑8 offsuit is a marginal but playable, connected hand with decent equity against a likely wide SB opening range. Flash has shown frequent preflop aggression, so calling keeps weaker hands in and gives me good pot odds (200 into a 400 pot ≈ 3:1) while keeping the pot manageable out of position. A 3‑bet here would be thin and I don’t want to invest more OOP with this holding. Plan: call to see a flop and proceed based on texture — continue with pairs or strong draws, fold to heavy aggression when completely missed.

I am the pre-flop aggressor and have flopped a Queen-high flush draw on a board of 6h-Jd-8d. A continuation bet is the standard play here to maintain initiative and attempt to take the pot down immediately. If called, I have significant equity to improve on the turn or river. I will bet 300, which is half the size of the pot.

I have 9d8s on 6h Jd 8d — middle pair (pair of eights) with a 9 kicker and only a very small runner‑runner diamond possibility. The pot is 900 and Flash bet 300; calling 300 gives me roughly 3:1 pot odds (I need about 25% equity). Flash has a wide preflop/c-bet range and will bet many flops, so my pair has decent showdown value and reasonable chance to improve to two pair/trips. Raising OOP here is thin and often dominated by Jx/sets; folding is too tight given the pot odds. I’ll call to realize equity and keep the pot controlled, then re-evaluate on the turn — be ready to fold to heavy turn/river aggression if I miss, or apply pressure if I improve or see weakness.

On the turn 5s, I still hold a Queen-high flush draw. The 5s is mostly a blank card that shouldn't have improved Minni's range significantly unless she's playing exactly 79 or a set. By continuing with a second barrel, I apply pressure on her marginal pairs (like 8x or 6x) and some of her own draws. If she calls, I still have roughly 20% equity to hit my flush on the river. I'll bet 800 into the 1200 pot to maintain initiative and maximize fold equity.

I hold 9♦8♠ on 6♥ J♦ 8♦ 5♠ — one pair (eights) with a 9 kicker. The pot is 2000 and Flash bet 800; calling 800 wins ~2800 (I need ≈29% equity). Flash has shown a wide SB opening/c-bet range, so his turn sizing can include Jx, small pairs, a lot of overcards, and some pure bluffs or single‑diamond hands. My pair beats a large chunk of that range and has modest river improvement chances (9s/8s to make two‑pair/trips ≈ ~9% combined), plus decent showdown value against floats. Raising OOP here is thin — I don’t want to build a bigger pot with a marginal hand and risk getting shoved or face domination by Jx/sets. Folding is too tight given the pot odds and the showdown value. Plan: call the 800, see the river, and re‑evaluate — call small/medium river bets on blanks, fold to large overbets or trips/flush displays, and consider a small value bet only if checked to on a harmless river.

I missed my flush draw but caught bottom pair on the river with the Deuce of hearts. While a pair of deuces is extremely weak, it does have some showdown value against missed straight draws like 9-7 or 9-10 which Minni might have called with on the flop and turn. Given that Minni has called two streets already, she likely has a piece of this board (like a Jack or an Eight) and is unlikely to fold to a third barrel unless I bet very large, which is risky. I will check to see if I can win a cheap showdown or fold if she puts in a significant bet.

Board is 6♥ J♦ 8♦ 5♠ 2♥; I hold 9♦8♠ — one pair (eights) with a decent kicker. Flash opened preflop and barreled flop (300) and turn (800) but then checked the river, which is consistent with a wide range of medium‑strength hands (Jx for thin value, missed overcards, floats) and some bluffs. My hand beats a large portion of that checking range but loses to Jx/sets. With the pot at 2800 a small value bet (~1/3 pot) is the best line: it extracts from worse A/K/other missed hands and thin calls, keeps the pot controlled, and minimizes loss vs a check‑raise. I don’t want to overcommit OOP — I’ll bet a small amount to get value and fold to significant aggression. Action: small value bet of 900.

On the river, the board completes as 6h-Jd-8d-5s-2h. I have improved to bottom pair with deuces, but this is a very weak hand in a pot where Minni has called two substantial bets. Minni's line (calling pre-flop, flop, and turn) suggests she has a piece of this board, likely a Jack, an eight, or a six. While the 900 bet into a 2800 pot offers me decent pot odds (calling 900 to win a total pot of 4600, requiring about 19.5% equity), it is unlikely that my pair of deuces is ahead. She is unlikely to call an 800-chip turn bet with a hand that my pair of deuces beats now, except for specifically missed straight draws like 10-9 or 10-7. Given her solid play, I expect her to show up with a better pair or a straight (9-7) more often than a total bluff. I will fold and preserve my stack.

I am on the button with 8d 2s. This is a very weak starting hand, often considered one of the worst in poker. Although I am in the best position, opening with such a weak hand into two active and aggressive opponents like Minni and Elon is not profitable. Given the tournament stage (Hand 131/150) and my stack size, there is no reason to risk chips on a high-frequency fold. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Heads-up from the small blind with K♠9♣ — a playable, initiative-taking hand. I want to avoid playing a limp out of position and seize momentum, so I’ll open-raise to a standard 3x sizing (300 total). This gives fold equity vs Elon’s wide BB defending range and lets me c-bet many flops when I take the lead. If he 3-bets heavily I’ll respect that and fold this marginal hand; if he just calls I’ll proceed aggressively on favorable boards (top pair, good draws) and fold when clearly behind.

In the big blind facing a small blind raise to 300 (facing 200 to call into a pot of 400), QTo offsuit is a playable hand with decent equity against a wide SB opening range, which is often stealing or light due to position. Pot odds are excellent (5:1), and I have position postflop. Calling preserves stack, allows realization of equity on favorable flops (straight draws, broadway potential), and avoids bloating the pot with a raise on a marginal hand. Folding is too tight given the price; raising risks isolation with a hand that plays better multiway or deepstack postflop. Plan: see flop, c-bet if hits strong, check-fold weak boards, look to float or bluff on dry boards if villain shows weakness.

I opened preflop with K♠9♣ and Elon (BB) just called. Flop 8♣ 4♣ T♣ gives me a strong four-flush (9 club outs) plus a K overcard — roughly ~35% equity to the river to make the flush (plus some extra equity to pair the K). As the preflop aggressor this is an ideal spot to semi-bluff: I want to take the initiative, deny correct odds to many of his continuing hands, and build the pot when I hit. A 2/3-pot c-bet (≈400 into the 600 pot) gives good fold equity while charging worse clubs and single-pair/overcard hands. If he folds I scoop; if he calls I still have ~35% to win by the river and can proceed based on turn texture; if he check-raises large I’ll heavily discount my K-high and likely fold to big sizing (it usually represents a made club or very strong hand), while a small check-raise can be called given my draw and deep stacks. Bet 400 is the balanced, +EV play here.

TPTK (top pair, top kicker with QhTd on Tc-8c-4c board) is very strong heads-up OOP vs SB who raised pre and c-bets 40% pot. Her range: wide continuation including Ax suited/clubby, broadway, pocket pairs, bluffs. Board is draw-heavy (clubs flush draw ~9% equity for flush draws, gutshots like 76s, 95s). Raising extracts value from worse pairs (like 99-JJ, weaker A/high), Ax no kicker, pure draws, while charging them heavily and denying equity. Flat calling invites free turns to draws or floats; folding is spew. Minni aggressive but stacks deep (12k effective). Raise sizing ~3.5x her bet to 1400 total builds pot OOP, sets up turn barrel if check-called. Plan: if she shoves/folds, cool; if calls, bet turn most boards unless scary.

Board is T♣-8♣-4♣ and I have K♠-9♣ — a strong club draw (9 clubs) plus a K overcard. I c-bet 400 as aggressor and Elon check‑raised to 1,400, leaving me to call 1,000 more. The pot is 2,400; calling 1,000 gives me ~29.4% pot odds (1,000 / 3,400). My flush-by-river equity with 9 outs is ~35% (plus some extra equity to pair the K), so a call is +EV versus a reasonably wide raising range. Folding surrenders too much equity for a relatively small investment; a reraise to represent a made flush would be thin and likely just gets called by better flushes. I’ll call 1,000 more (1400 total committed on the flop) and reevaluate on the turn — fold to extreme strength (big shove) if I miss and face heavy action, but continue aggressively if I hit the flush or pick up a K.

Situation: I’m heads-up with K♠9♣. Preflop I opened to 300 and Elon called. Flop came 8♣-4♣-T♣; I c-bet 400, he check‑raised to 1,400 and I called. Turn is 4♠, pot now 3,400 and it’s checked to me. Assessment: my hand is still just a 4‑to‑a‑flush (one club in hand, three on board) with 9 outs to hit the river: 9/46 ≈ 19.6% (plus a tiny extra equity from pairing the K). Elon’s flop check‑raise strongly polarizes his range toward made clubs or very strong hands — not an easy spot to build the pot with only ~20% to improve. The turn pairing the board also increases full‑house possibilities for him if he held a 4. Plan: avoid bloating the pot with a marginal draw and let him show more. I’ll control the pot by checking the turn and see the river for free; if he bets a small river I can call with pot odds or shove/raise if I hit. If he leads big into this check on the turn I’ll treat it as significant strength and fold to oversized pressure.

Facing Minni's check on the turn after she called my flop raise OOP with QhTd for TPTK on a Tc8c4c4s board.
