The turn pairs the 4, which is somewhat scary, but my hand remains strong against her likely range: worse pairs (AQ/KQ/AJ, 99-JJ), stubborn Ax/club draws that missed or improved weakly, 84/T4 two-pair combos less likely given her flat call. She check-called flop raise as OOP raiser, suggesting capping range somewhat, unlikely sets/trips that slowplay twice. Betting extracts value from worse made hands and charges any remaining spade/club draws (board has 3 clubs + spade). Pot 3400 deep stacks (~10k effective), 2200 bet (~65% pot) balances value/protection, gets calls from worse pairs/draws, sets up river value/shove if called. Checking risks free river improvements or pot control issues OOP for her on river; bet denies equity and builds pot with strong hand.

Timeline: I opened to 300 preflop, c‑bet the 8♣-4♣-T♣ flop for 400, Elon check‑raised to 1,400 and I called (pot after the flop = 3,400). I checked the turn (4♠) to control the pot; Elon now bets 2,200 into the 3,400 (pot = 5,600) so I must call 2,200. Equity/math: I have K♠9♣ — nine clubs to make a flush on the river = 9/46 ≈ 19.6%. Two non‑club kings are additional clean pair outs (at best) → 11/46 ≈ 23.9% total. Break‑even equity to call is 2,200 / (5,600+2,200) ≈ 28.2%. My realistic equity is below that and is likely lower once you account for Elon's line: a flop check‑raise then a turn lead is heavily polarized to made clubs or very strong value hands (TT/88/etc.). Many club rivers that help me will still lose to higher club holdings. Raising as a bluff is thin — he has shown strength and will call or shove a lot of my bluffs, and I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal draw. Sunk cost aside, folding preserves a healthy stack for better spots. Action: fold.

I’m on the button with 9♠8♦ — a playable, positionally advantaged but marginal hand. The blinds have only posted (pot 150, to-call 100), so I want to take the initiative and try to steal the dead money and play the flop in position. A standard button open to 300 (3x the big blind) gives decent fold equity and keeps weaker hands from seeing the flop for cheap. If I get called I’ll proceed cautiously with positional advantage; if I face a sizable 3-bet I’ll fold this marginal offsuit hand rather than get involved out of position.

Preflop in SB with ATo facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB). ATo has excellent equity against a wide button opening range, dominating hands like A9-A2, KQo, QJo, suited connectors, etc. Minni raises frequently from button based on history, suggesting a light range. Position is poor, but 3-betting isolates, builds pot with strong hand, and applies pressure on BB (Flash) who defends variably. Deep stacks (140+ BB) allow postflop playability if called. Folding is too tight; flat-calling OOP risks multiway pot with dominated hand. Standard 3-bet size ~3x opponent's raise: to 900 total. Applies fold equity while getting value.

In Hand #132, I am in the Big Blind with Queen-Ten offsuit (QTo). Minni (Button) opened for 300, and Elon (Small Blind) 3-bet to 900. Facing a raise and a 3-bet, QTo is significantly behind the likely ranges of both opponents, especially Elon's 3-betting range from the small blind. Being out of position for the rest of the hand against two aggressive players makes this a clear fold to preserve my stack for better opportunities, as I still have a healthy 116 BBs.

Facing a 3‑bet to 900 on the button with 9♠8♦ (offsuit) — a marginal but playable hand in position — I prefer to call. The extra cost to see a flop is only 600 (bringing my total commitment to 900), which is a small fraction of my 13.7k stack and gives me position versus Elon postflop. Folding surrenders good positional leverage; 4‑betting with this hand is spewy and unnecessary. Pot odds on the call are roughly 600/(1300+600) ≈ 31–32%, and versus an aggressive/loose 3‑bet range I can expect usable equity plus postflop playability. Plan: call, see the flop, and proceed cautiously — fold to a large 4‑bet, c‑bet or float depending on the flop texture and Elon's tendencies, and use position to realize equity or apply pressure when appropriate.

Heads-up on the flop from SB after 3-betting preflop with ATo vs Minni's BTN open and flat-call. Board is 3c-9d-Js rainbow, very dry with limited draws (gutshots like QT, T8; open-enders unlikely). My range is strong: top pair/top kicker often (AJs/AKo), overpairs (QQ+), sets. ATo has Ace-high + gutshot straight draw to Q (A-Q-J-T-9 straight), ~4% equity boost + fold equity. Minni's calling range lighter: 88-JJ, Axs (A9-A2 suited/off), suited connectors (QTs, T8s, 87s), broadways (KQs, QJo). I block AA/AK/AQ. C-bet for value/thin value from weaker Ax/pairs and protection/fold equity vs floats/draws. Board favors my range. Pot 1900, bet ~2/3 pot (1300) balances value/protection while keeping pot manageable OOP with deep stacks. Avoid checking to induce bluffs/keep pot small OOP.

I have 9♠8♦ on a 3♣-9♦-J♠ flop — that’s a pair of nines (second pair) in position. Elon 3‑bet preflop, so his range is weighted toward strong hands (AJ/AQ/AK, KQ, TT+, maybe some bluffs), and his 1300 C‑bet into a 3200 pot is a moderate continuation bet. Calling 1300 gives me ~29% pot odds and preserves positional advantage while keeping the pot under control with a vulnerable but valuable hand. Raising here would bloat the pot with a hand that fares poorly against Jx/overpairs and risks folding out worse hands only to be called by better. Folding cedes too much equity. Plan: call to see the turn, use position to evaluate — fold to heavy double‑barrels on very scary cards or large raises, call smaller turn bets, and extract value if I improve or get a favorable runout.

Turn board 3c-9d-Js-2c features a completed low-end straight draw (e.g., T-Q already had equity, 4-5 gutshot improved slightly) and flush draw with two clubs. A♥T♠ holds Ace-high showdown value (~20-30% vs calling range) with blockers to top Ax (AA/AK/AQ/AJ/AT), but no pair/draw itself. Preflop 3-bet + flop c-bet reps polarized strong range (sets/overpairs/Jx/97/AJ+/draws). Minni's BTN open-call OOP + flop call: pairs 77-JJ/87-98-T9 (blocks my sets), Jx (JT/J9/J8 suited), combo draws (QTs/T8s suited clubs), Ax suited clubs. Her range hits ~40% (pairs/Jx), but many medium strength that check/call OOP. OOP first to act, deep stacks (pot 4500, SPR ~2.5): betting risks call from all pairs/Jx/draws, bad on river; thin value poor as Ace-high behind. Check exploits her aggression (history: Minni bets rivers/value thin), induces bluffs from missed draws (club flush whiffed low cards), realizes equity vs value, pot control. Call small bets; fold large. Optimal balance post c-bet.

I have 9♠8♦ — second pair (9s) on a relatively dry 3♣-9♦-J♠-2♣ board and I’m in position. Elon 3‑bet pre and c‑bet the flop but then checked the turn, which looks like weakness or pot control rather than a clear value line. This is a good spot to take the initiative: a turn bet folds out missed overcards from his 3‑bet range (AK/AQ/KQ, some Kx/Qx), charges any backdoor club/spade draws and extracts value from worse made hands that will still call (small pairs, some weak Jx/calling hands). I don’t want to bloat the pot with a vulnerable second‑pair hand, nor do I want to give a free river card, so I’ll bet a medium size — 2,000 into a 4,500 pot (~44% pot). This size is big enough to get folds and preserve fold equity but small enough that I can comfortably fold to a strong check‑raise. If Elon check‑raises strongly, I’ll assume he has a very strong hand (Jx/sets/overpairs) and release; if he calls I’ll re‑evaluate the river and proceed cautiously to protect my stack.

Turn check was to pot control OOP with Ace-high and induce bluffs from Minni's aggressive BTN range after calling 3-bet/flop OOP on a dry board. Now facing ~44% pot bet (2000 into 4500), which sizes down from flop call expectation. My A♥T♠ blocks AJ/AQ/AT (key Jx value), has ~25-35% equity vs her range: Jx (JT/J9/J8, ~20% of range, behind), pairs/draws (99/TT?/88-77, behind but thin), clubs (AcXc/TcXc/5c4c, live often if low), missed floats/semi-bluffs (87s/T8s/QTs clubs/off). Beats bluffs/missed draws (~40-50% range). Excellent pot odds (2000 into 6500 = 3.25:1, need 23%). Deep stacks post-call (pot ~8500, SPR ~1:1) favors calling to realize equity/check river OOP (control pot, check-fold river jams/bets, check-call thin value). Raise thin (min 4000 risks stack-off behind vs Jx/sets); fold too tight vs history of Minni thin value OOP (e.g., Hand 25/50/130). Call balances check-call range.

River board 3c-9d-Js-2c-2s pairs the deuces low on the river, filling low gutshots (e.g., 4-5 missed but T-Q straight possible though narrow range, any flush draws missed as only two clubs and rainbow otherwise). Heads-up OOP first to act after 3-bet pre, c-bet flop, check-call turn bet into ~4500 pot (SPR was ~2.5 pre-turn bet, now post-call pot 8500, effective stacks ~10k/SPR ~1.2). AhTs holds pure Ace-high (blocks AJ/AQ/AT top Ax combos in her range) with ~30-40% equity vs Minni's polarized BTN OOP calling range: value (Jx ~JT/J9/Js, 99 overpair, 22+ sets/trips, any two now), thin value/semis from turn (club draws AcXc/5c4c live-ish but blocked/missed, T8s/87s for gutshots now pairing low). Beats bluffs/missed draws (~40-50%: QTs offsuit floats, lower pairs 88-44 folding turn). Betting Ace-high thin value risks getting called by all Jx/99/22+ (~50% range crushes us, pot equity poor) while folding out bluffs we beat; deepish SPR allows but OOP minimizes mistakes. Check realizes equity (beats her bluffs/caps at showdown), induces bets from thin value/aggression (Minni history: rivers Hand 25/50/78/130 betting thin OOP/value pots), pot control OOP (call river bet maximizes EV if small, check-fold large jams/overbets). Raise/fold off table. Optimal check exploits range advantage post my check-call rep strength while denying her free showdown.

River decision with 9♠8♦ on 3♣-9♦-J♠-2♣-2♠: I have second pair (pair of nines) and we raised the turn to 2,000 and got a call from Elon's 3‑bet range. That line (3‑bet pre, c‑bet flop, check turn, call our raise) looks like medium strength hands (Jx/9x, some overpairs and occasional bluffs). The river paired the board with a deuce, which gives trips to any deuce in his hand — a real but relatively rare combo vs his 3‑betting range. Elon checked to me, showing some passivity/weakness or a desire to pot‑control. Checking behind would lock in showdown value but surrenders thin value from hands that will call a small river bet (Jx, 9x, some Qx/Kx). I prefer to take a small value bet here to extract from those dominated or worse hands while keeping risk controlled. A 2,000 river bet into an 8,500 pot is small enough to get called by worse but not so large that I overcommit; if Elon check‑raises big it’s almost always a very strong hand (sets/trips/boats) and I will fold. Plan: bet 2,000 for thin value; fold to a significant raise, and show down if called.

River board pairs the deuces (3c9dJs2c2s), completing no new draws (spade flush unlikely without prior rep, clubs bricked after turn club). AhTs holds pure ace-high blocker to key Ax holdings (AJs/AQo/ATo/AJx) in Minni's OOP calling range post-3bet/flop c-bet/turn check-call, which is polarized: value (Jx ~25-30%: JTs/J9s/J8s/JQo etc., 99 overpair, 22/32 trips/set ~10%, thin 9x/32 ~10%) vs bluffs/missed draws/semis (~50-60%: turn club draws AcXc/QcTc etc. now bricked, gutshots T8/87/ Q10 missed, floats/lower pairs 88-44 betting thin OOP). Beats all bluffs/lower pairs/A-hi worse, ~35% equity. Pot odds exceptional (2000 to win 12500 total pot = ~14% required). Calling realizes equity OOP at low cost (SPR post-call ~0.6, stacks committed variably but pot grows favorably if raised which she won't with air). Folding too tight vs Minni's history of thin river value OOP (Hands 25/50/78/109/130 betting rivers thin OOP/value pots with medium strength); raising risks stack-off behind Jx/22 while folding equity low vs value-heavy capping range. Call balances check-call range (trips/99/Jx slowplay/check value), exploits her aggression post my check strength rep.

3-handed on the button with deep stacks. J4o is offsuit, poorly connected, and low cards with little playability. Preflop first to act, standard range even from button should exclude this trash hand. Folding preserves stack for premium spots; no need to speculate with such a weak holding.

I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Five offsuit (J5o). With the Button (Elon) folding, it is a blind-versus-blind situation against Minni, the current chip leader. J5o is a very weak hand that plays poorly out of position. Minni has shown significant aggression throughout the tournament and is unlikely to let me steal the blinds easily. At this stage of the tournament (Hand 133 of 150) and with a healthy stack, there is no need to enter a pot with such a marginal hand from an unfavorable position.

I am on the button with 7c 4s, which is a very weak starting hand. Given the stack sizes and the playing styles of Minni and Elon, who have both shown aggression recently, there is no strategic reason to enter the pot with such a marginal hand. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I'm in the small blind with A♠5♥. Pot is 150 and I only need to put in 50 to see a flop, but heads-up A5o is strong enough to open-raise to seize initiative and exploit the big blind's wide defending range. Raising to 200 total is a standard, inexpensive raise that gives me fold equity, uses the Ace blocker, and lets me play the pot on my terms. Plan: fold or seriously re-evaluate to a large 3-bet; if called I will c-bet favorable flops and proceed with top-pair/Ace boards or control the pot otherwise.

3d9c is a trash hand: offsuit, low cards with a 6-card gap, poor playability, low showdown value. Facing a min-raise from aggressive SB Minni (who raises frequently per history), her range is wide but still crushes this. Calling 100 into ~400 pot (4:1 odds) not justified given reverse implied odds and position advantage insufficient. Late tournament (134/150), preserve stack for premium spots vs short-handed play. Clear fold to maximize EV long-term.

Three-handed on the button is a great spot to open a wide range. J♣6♦ is marginal, but I have deep stacks (204 BB) and positional advantage against only two blinds, so I want to take the initiative. I’ll open-raise to 3x the big blind (300) to apply pressure, deny free flops, and give myself postflop leverage. If I get one caller I’ll c-bet favorable/dry textures and play aggressively in position; if I face a substantial 3-bet I’ll fold this hand (it’s easily dominated). This sizing balances fold equity and keeps the pot manageable with a marginal holding.

3h4c is a complete trash hand with no playability, low cards, offsuit, poor equity against any reasonable button raising range from Minni, who has been aggressive but this doesn't change the fundamentals. From SB, facing a raise, pot odds are about 1.8:1 requiring ~35% equity which this hand lacks. Standard fold to preserve stack late in tournament with 16 hands left.

I am in the Big Blind with Jack-Six offsuit. Minni, the chip leader, has raised from the Button to 300. While I am getting decent pot odds to call (200 to win a pot that will be 650), J6o is a very weak hand to play out of position against an aggressive chip leader. With only 15 hands remaining in the tournament, I prefer to wait for a better spot to commit chips rather than defending with a marginal hand that is easily dominated.

3-handed on the button with Jc8d offsuit. This is a speculative hand with straight potential and decent high card value. SB (Flash) likely limped (pot 150 to call 100), BB (Minni) posted. Excellent position postflop. Standard to raise from button vs limped pot to isolate and build pot with position. Opponents deep-stacked; Minni aggressive historically, Flash calls light sometimes. Raise to 300 (3x BB) for value/thin value and fold equity. Folding too tight 3-handed; calling OOP risky.

In Hand #136, I am in the Small Blind holding Queen of Spades and Seven of Hearts. Elon, on the Button, has opened with a raise to 300. Q7o is a weak hand to defend from the Small Blind, especially since I would be out of position for the remainder of the hand. Given that we are late in the tournament (Hand 136 of 150) and I have a healthy stack of over 100 big blinds, there is no need to make a marginal or negative expectation play with a subpar hand. Folding here is the most disciplined and standard professional decision to preserve chips for more favorable opportunities.

Facing a button open to 300 from one opponent while I'm in the big blind with Kc Qh, I want to take the initiative rather than play a marginal, out-of-position pot. KQ is strong enough to 3-bet as part of a value/mix range versus a wide button opening; a ~3x sizing (to 900 total) gives meaningful fold equity, charges speculative hands, and keeps the pot manageable given effective stacks (I’m ~204 BB, Elon ~76 BB). Plan: raise to 900 to isolate/steal preflop. If Elon 4-bets large, I’ll respect that strength and fold to avoid getting dominated; if he calls I’ll c-bet appropriate textures and proceed postflop with pot control.

Facing a 3-bet to 900 (600 to call into ~1850 pot) from Minni in the BB with Jc8d offsuit on the button. While position is excellent postflop and Minni has shown aggression, J8o is a marginal speculative hand with gaps, no suits, and poor equity against a typical 3-bet range (even loose). Implied odds are decent deep-stacked (76BB), but reverse implied odds are high if behind. Calling risks playing OOP effectively postflop if multiway or dominated. 4-betting is a bluff with too weak a hand and poor blockers. Standard fold preserves stack for premium spots in late tournament 3-handed. Initial raise was thin positionally, but fold to resistance.

I am on the button with 82o, which is one of the weakest possible hands. Although I am in a late position and could theoretically attempt a steal, the risk-to-reward ratio is poor here. Minni (the big stack) has been playing aggressively, and Elon (the short stack) is at a depth where he might frequently shove over a button raise. With 13 hands left in the tournament and a healthy stack, there is no need to commit chips with trash.

Holding 9♠3♦ in the small blind is a very weak, unconnected offsuit hand with poor playability out of position. Pot is 150 and I must call 50 more (or raise to 200). Elon in the big blind defends widely and can apply pressure — a raise will often be called or 3-bet and will put me in difficult postflop spots with little equity. Calling 50 to see a flop is cheap but still leaves me OOP with a dominated hand. With a healthy stack and only a handful of hands left in the tournament, the prudent, +EV play is to fold and preserve chips for better spots.

J♦3♦ is a weak hand but suited and has decent playability on the button in a 3‑handed pot. Calling 100 is cheap relative to my 20,870 stack and lets me use position to see a flop without committing a lot. A min‑raise to 200 is too small to credibly steal and would just invite 3‑bets from the aggressive blinds, so I prefer to see the flop and play post‑flop in position. Plan: continue aggressively if I hit (pair, two pair, strong diamond draw), otherwise check and fold to large pressure — fold to meaningful preflop 3‑bets.

Q℃ 9♠ is a playable speculative hand with straight potential and queen high. Facing a limp from Minni on the button, who has a large stack and has shown loose tendencies in past hands (e.g., frequent calls and limps). Pot is 250, calling adds only 50 for great implied odds (~5:1 direct, plus implied). Folding too tight in SB; raising OOP risks bloating pot with marginal strength against wide button limp range. Flash in BB may check or raise, but prefer cheap flop to realize equity. Late tournament stage, preserve stack but exploit limp.

I am in the Big Blind with a weak hand (Qh 4d). The Button and Small Blind have both limped into the pot. There is no reason to raise and build a pot out of position with a sub-par hand. Checking allows me to see a free flop and potentially improve or find a spot to outplay my opponents later if the board texture allows.

Flop is 8s As Ks, a highly coordinated monotone spade board. My Qc9s gives me the strong nut flush draw (9-high flush draw with A/K/8 on board, solid equity). No straight draw or pair, but ~35-40% equity vs typical calling ranges. Three players to the flop after limps/check, wide ranges likely, many weaker draws, Ax, Kx, pairs possible. OOP in SB first to act. Betting as semi-bluff: fold equity vs missed hands/draws, value from worse (lower draws, pairs), denies equity, builds pot with my ~9 outs (18% turn, 35% by river). Check risks multiway pot, free card to BB/Button, or check-raise spot but betting proactive in late tournament ICM pressure (3 left, my 7.5k M~25, preserve but grow stack). Size ~2/3 pot (200 into 300) balanced, allows fold/call/raise options. Minni loose-aggressive history (big calls/raises), Flash variable; exploit wide limp ranges.

The flop is extremely coordinated with three spades (As Ks 8s). I have no spades, no pairs, and no potential draws. Elon has led out with a bet of 200 into a 300 pot, and Minni is still to act behind me. Given that I have virtually zero equity on this board and no reasonable way to win the hand without a high-risk bluff, I will fold.

I completely missed this flop. Board 8♠ A♠ K♠ is very ugly for J♦3♦ — no spade, no pair and no legitimate backdoor. I only have 6 outs (three J’s and three 3’s) — roughly ~24–25% equity to improve by the river. Elon’s raise to 200 into the ~500 pot puts ~700 to play, so calling 200 only gives ~22–23% break‑even equity. His line is consistent with value (A/K or a spade), which dominate me heavily, so this is a marginal spot to chase. I’ll preserve my deep stack and fold rather than make a loose call or a risky bluff on a three‑spade board.

3-handed on the button with J9s suited connectors: excellent position, high playability for straights/flushes, strong speculative hand late in tournament. Pot 150, to-call only 100 offers great implied odds for a call, but raising takes initiative, isolates potentially weak ranges from SB/BB, builds pot when ahead vs loose callers like Minni, and applies pressure given Flash's frequent folds to aggression seen in history (e.g., many folds pre/post). Min-raise to 200 too small; standard 3x facing small bet is 300 total, denying equity to worse hands/drawing hands. My stack ~79BB effective, deep enough to maneuver postflop. Goal: maximize stack by winning pots uncontested or value-betting.

Holding 6c 3c in the Small Blind against a Button raise from Elon is a clear fold. The hand is extremely weak and playing out of position against two active opponents is not profitable. With a comfortable stack of 11,290, there is no need to defend with sub-par holdings.

I’m in the big blind with 8♥5♥ facing a standard button open to 300 (pot 450). I only need to put in 200 more (300 total) to continue. With Elon’s effective stack (~75 bb) this is deep enough that suited 8-5 has good playability and implied odds against a wide BTN opening range. Pot odds: calling 200 into a 450 pot (650 total after my call) requires ~30.8% equity to break even — realistic given his wide opening range and my flush/straight potential. 3-betting OOP with 8-5 suited is too thin (and the min‑raise to 500 would be a weak, marginal reraise). Folding is overly tight. Plan: call, see a flop, and proceed aggressively when I pick up hearts/strong draws or play cautiously on dry broadway/A-high boards.

I have top pair (8) with a weak 5-kicker on 8♦7♣2♣ and I’m first to act on the flop. Elon opened a wide BTN range preflop so I’m often ahead vs his continuation range, but my kicker is vulnerable and there are a couple of backdoor club/straight possibilities. I want to extract value from worse pairs (7x, 2x, small pockets) and charge any club/connector combos while keeping the pot controlled versus his ~75bb stack. A half‑pot bet accomplishes that — big enough to get value/protection but not so large it commits me vs a big raise. Bet 325; re-evaluate if Elon raises (call small raises, fold or pot‑control vs large sizing).

3-handed button vs BB donk bet on 872 rainbow flop (two low clubs). Preflop raiser with J9hh: missed but gutshot straight draw (needs T for nuts straight), live J overcard, backdoor heart flush draw, excellent blockers to strong hands (no 87 suited, etc.). Pot 975, bet 325 (~33% pot), great spot for semi-bluff raise: position, initiative back, Minni's history shows she calls pre but folds postflop to aggression often (e.g., Hand 25 river call small but many folds like 21,30,51). Raising ~3x to 975 denies equity to her draws/pairs, gets value from worse, fold equity high (~50% likely fold), implied odds deep stacks if called. Fold too nitty missing dead money; flat floats but vulnerable multiway if Flash wakes up (unlikely). Max EV: raise semi-bluff to take pot or continue.

I have top pair (8) with a weak 5‑kicker on 8♦7♣2♣. I bet 325 as Bb to get value/protection and Elon raised to 975, leaving me 650 to call. Calling 650 into a 1950 pot requires only ~25% equity (650/(1950+650)=25%), which is cheap given Elon’s wide BTN opening and his raise will include bluffs/club draws and worse pairs that I beat. I have no club draw and my kicker is vulnerable, so 3‑betting/raising (min to 1625) is too thin — it bloats the pot and commits me with a marginal hand. Plan: call 650 (total 975) to see the turn and re‑evaluate — fold to very large turn shove on obvious completed draws/sets, but call modest turn bets to extract value from worse pairs and to continue against bluffs.

Turn: I improved to two pair (8s & 5s) on 8♦ 7♣ 2♣ 5♣. That’s a substantial pickup versus Elon's flop‑raising range — he can have sets or a completed club flush, but those combos are relatively rare (two‑club hole combos that make a flush on turn are only a few percent of combinations given the known cards). Most of his flop‑raise range (single pairs, pair+draws, bluffs) is now behind me. My objective is to extract value from worse pairs (7x, 2x, single pocket pairs) and charge club/connector hands that will still call, while keeping the pot controlled against the small fraction of hands that beat me. Betting about half the pot (~1300 into 2600) accomplishes that: it prices worse hands to call, denies free cards that could give him a full house, and keeps me from being over‑committed if he turns out to have a nut flush and raises big. Plan: bet 1300 for value; if Elon check‑raises a small/medium amount I will mostly call and re‑evaluate on the river, but I will fold to a very large shove that strongly represents a completed flush/set.

Turn board 8d7c2c5c (three clubs, straight draw possible with 4-6 or 6-9 now). J9h has gutshot to T for broadway (4 outs ~8%), backdoor hearts, J overcard. Minni's line OOP: called pre raise, donk flop small 872cc, called my large iso-raise 975, now leads 1300 into ~2600 pot (~50% pot). This is a strong line indicating pairs+draws, sets (88/77/22), two-pair (87s/75? but 5 turn), completed club flushes unlikely (only three clubs, she'd raise flop), or improved draws like 46/69 clubs/straight draws. History shows Minni calls pre OOP often but folds to postflop aggression frequently; however, calling flop raise then leading turn filters to value-heavy range. Pot odds 1300/5200 ~28% equity needed, but vs her capped strong range equity ~15-20% max (gutshot crushes to sets/flushes). Call vulnerable to river bet/raise OOP lead likely; raise lacks fold equity deep (~18k stack) vs committed range, thin value. Late tourney (139/150, 3-left), preserve ~66BB stack (6615) for better spots, avoid spew on marginal draw vs polarized strong line. Fold best EV to realize fold equity from flop semi-bluff.

I am on the button with Jack-Seven offsuit. While this is a 3-handed game where opening ranges are wider, J7o is a marginal hand that doesn't play particularly well post-flop, especially against aggressive opponents like Minni and Elon who have shown a willingness to 3-bet or play large pots recently. Given the current stack dynamics and the aggressiveness of the blinds, folding is the most disciplined play to avoid being put in a difficult spot with a mediocre hand.

We're heads-up vs the big blind and holding Q♦J♣ — a strong, playable hand in the small blind. Limping is too passive (gives BB cheap odds and surrenders initiative). I want to seize the initiative, isolate Elon and get value from his wide calling range, while keeping the pot size reasonable given deep stacks. A standard open to 300 (common table sizing) achieves that: it gives fold equity and builds a pot when I'm likely ahead, but isn't so large that a 3-bet commits me unnecessarily. If he 3-bets big I can fold or reevaluate; if he calls I have a good hand to continue postflop. Raising to 300 is the best balance of value and pressure here.

54 suited is a strong defensive hand in the big blind against a small blind raise, especially after the button folds. Excellent implied odds if I hit a straight or flush draw. Pot odds are favorable: calling 200 into a pot of 400 (3:1 direct, better implied). Minni's raise to 300 (3x) is standard but her range is wide from SB. Out of position but deep stacks allow maneuvering postflop. Folding is too tight; raising is overplaying trash. Call to see flop and play in position dynamics.

I have top pair (Q♥) with a decent kicker on a fairly dry Q♠‑7♦‑5♠ flop. Elon called my preflop raise from the big blind, so his range is wide (worse Qx, small pairs, some broadway hands and occasional two‑card spade combos) — very few hands reliably beat me (sets, rare two‑pair). I want to extract value and deny equity while keeping the pot controlled against his ~63BB stack. A half‑pot continuation bet (~300 into 600) gets called by worse queens and many medium pairs/draws, keeps initiative, and lets me fold or re‑evaluate comfortably if he shows strong aggression.

Flop gives me bottom pair (5s) with a gutshot straight draw to the 6, turning the hand into 4-5-6-7 straight. Board is Qs-7d-5s, two spades, but I have backdoor spade draw with 4s. Facing a 1/3 pot c-bet (300 into 900) from Minni's wide SB opening range after I defended BB preflop. Direct pot odds are 3:1 (25% equity needed), and my hand has ~30-40% equity against her polarized range (overpairs, Qx, Ax suited, draws). Implied odds are strong with my 6315 stack vs her massive 21k; if I hit, she pays off. OOP but can check-fold turn if misses or check-raise if improves. Folding bottom pair + gutshot OOP to small bet is too tight in deepstacked spot; raising is bluff-heavy and risks stack-off OOP. Call to realize equity and control pot size.

Turn brings 9♠ and now the board is 7♦ Q♠ 5♠ 9♠ — three spades on board. I hold Q♥ J♣: top pair with a decent kicker but zero spade protection. Elon cold‑called my flop c‑bet, so his range contains Qx, midpairs and a lot of spade draws. The turn completes many of those draws into made flushes. Betting the turn (a double barrel) would bloat the pot and get me raised/shoved by completed spades or called by more marginal hands I already beat — it’s high risk with limited extra fold equity. I prefer pot control and information: check and let Elon show his hand. If he bets a small/medium size I can call and extract value from worse queens and pairs; if he jams or puts in heavy aggression I can fold and preserve a large stack vs a polarized range that likely has the flush.

Turn board brings the 9s, completing three spades on board (Qs, 5s, 9s) and giving me the nut flush draw with my 4s (9 outs), plus my bottom pair of fives remains good kicker/draw combo. Still have a gutshot to the 6 for a straight (4 outs, some overlap). Equity is very high: ~45-50% against Minni's SB continuation range (Qx, overpairs like TT+, missed Ax suited, bluffs). She checked turn after small flop c-bet, which could be weakness (missed draw, air), trap with strong hand (set, two-pair), or inducing. Pot 1200, stacks deep (my 6k ~5x pot, her 21k massive). OOP but her check gives me initiative. Checking realizes equity cheaply but gives free card to her draws/overcards. Betting semi-bluffs: denies equity to her spade draws/KQ/AT, builds pot with my equity, gets value from Qx/77/55/T9 etc., folds out worse like overcards/A-high. If she raises, I have fold equity or can jam draw. Sizing 800 (~2/3 pot) balances value and pressure without overbetting. Folding/checking passive; goal to maximize stack by applying pressure with huge draw in position dynamics.

The turn completed a three‑spade board and I checked to keep the pot manageable with Q♥J♣ (top pair, medium kicker, no spade). Elon cold‑called preflop and called my flop c‑bet, so his range here contains Qx and medium pairs plus a reasonable number of spade draws that are now completed. His 800 into a 2000 pot is a relatively small probe (~40% pot) that can be either value (Qx, some pairs) or a made/semi‑bluff with spades.

Raising here is risky: it bloats the pot when I’m often behind to a flush and only gets folds from the weakest parts of his range. Calling keeps the pot under control, gives me the correct pot odds (~28.5% break‑even vs the immediate pot) to realize equity, and lets me evaluate the river before committing more. If a non‑spade river comes I can extract value or call a reasonable bet; if a spade comes and he fires big, I can fold. So I’ll call the 800 to see the river.

Check. I have top pair (Qh) with a medium kicker on a three‑spade board that completed on the turn. Elon’s line (call flop c‑bet, then a turn bet of 800) is consistent with a polarized range that contains a lot of completed spade flushes plus some value Qx and medium pairs. The river 6♥ is a harmless blank for spades and doesn’t change the fact that many hands that continue this line beat me. Betting or raising here would bloat the pot when I’m often behind and would mainly get called by hands that already beat me; it would also narrow my fold equity against a polarized opponent. Checking keeps the pot under control, allows Elon to bluff or value‑bet thinly (which I can call), and preserves a large portion of my stack versus a likely made range. If Elon bets a small/medium probe on the river I can call for value; if he overbets or jams I can fold.

River 6h bricks my nut spade flush draw but pairs the board lowly and completes some straight draws like 8x (5-6-7-8-9). I have bottom pair of 5s with a 4 kicker, which has showdown value against Minni's SB range that bet flop small (continuation/weak Qx/draws), checked turn (weakness or trap), and called my semi-bluff turn bet 800 into 1200 (committed to showdown with Qx, mid pairs like 99/77/66, Ax spades missed flush, JT/QT str draws now with open ender? but river not helping all). Her river check after calling turn suggests not ultra strong (no check-raise), likely mid strength or missed draw wanting showdown. Pot 2800 (my stack 5215 ~52BB, her 20k+ covered), late in tournament (140/150), OOP but initiative from turn bet. Checking induces showdown and wins sometimes vs air/high card, but value betting thin extracts from her sticky calling range (Qx top pair, 99 two pair-ish, 77 set slowplayed, worse pairs/draws). Half-pot 1400 sizes well: builds pot to 4200, she calls off with better showdown equity hands, folds worse air; if raised, can fold profitably having realized equity earlier. Maximizes EV by charging her wide range in deep spot vs stationy opponent history (calls multiple bets streets often e.g. hand 25,50). Folding impossible, checking suboptimal value denial.

Call 1400.

Why: Elon bet 1400 into the pot that was ~2800 on the river. Calling 1400 to see showdown requires roughly 25% equity (1400 / (2800 + 2*1400) ≈ 25%). I have top pair (Q♥) with a decent J kicker — vulnerable to completed spade flushes (the turn put three spades on board) but ahead of a lot of hands in his range: non-spade Kx/Ax, medium single pairs, and missed draws that will still barrel. Elon's line (cold-call pre, call flop, lead on the turn, a ~50% pot river stab) is polarized — it contains value flushes and Qx/medium pairs but also bluffs and thin value bets I beat.

Folding gives up too much value against worse hands and would be too nitty given the pot odds. Raising is thin and risky: it bloats the pot when many of Elon's continuing hands are flushes (which will call or shove) and I don't want to get committed out of position with a vulnerable one-pair hand. Calling keeps the pot controlled, extracts value from worse hands that continue, and lets me fold to clear over-aggression if he jams. Therefore I call the 1400.

I’m on the button with AcQc — a strong, playable hand in position with two opponents. Pot is 150 and it’s 100 to call; min-raise is 200. I want to take the initiative, build the pot with position, and apply pressure to the SB (short stack) and BB. A standard 3x-ish open to 300 is the right sizing: it gives good fold equity, keeps weaker hands and blinds honest, and keeps the pot manageable if the SB shoves (Elon’s stack is only 3765). Postflop I’ll c-bet favorable Ace/Queen or dry boards, control the pot on highly coordinated boards, and re-evaluate versus 3-bets or shoves. Raise to 300.

Pocket Aces is the best starting hand in Hold'em. Facing a button raise from Minni, who often opens wide from late position based on history (frequent 300 raises), I should 3-bet aggressively to isolate, build the pot with my nuts, and charge draws or worse hands. My stack is 3765 (~37BB), effective against Flash's 11k but Minni deep. Raise sizing to 1200 total bet (4x the open) is standard for value/protection. BB may fold or call light, setting up postflop advantage.

I am holding 9c 3h in the Big Blind, which is a trash hand. Minni opened from the button and Elon 3-bet from the Small Blind. Elon is quite short-stacked with only about 26 big blinds total and has committed nearly half of his stack already. Regardless of whether Elon is value betting or shoving for survival, my hand has very little equity against his range or the original opener's range. Folding is the only sensible move to preserve my 11k stack for better opportunities in the final 10 hands.

I opened to 300 on the button with AcQc and SB (Elon) 3‑bet to 1200. Pot is ~1,600 and calling would cost me 900 more (total commit 1,200). Elon is short (2,615 ≈26bb effective) so his 3‑bet range is either value or polarized — but because he left chips behind I can apply pressure. AQs is a strong continuing hand in position and also blocks some of his best combos (many AK/AA combos). 4‑betting to the minimum (2,100 total) is the best line: it gets maximum fold equity vs his marginal 3‑bets, reduces his effective stack if he calls (he'd have only ~515 left), and still leaves me comfortable calling a shove because AQs has decent equity vs his shove/call range. Folding is too tight; flatting surrenders too much fold equity. I’ll 4‑bet to isolate and either take the pot down now or play a high‑equity hand in position against a short stack.

Pocket Aces remain the nuts preflop. Minni's 4-bet to 2100 after my 3-bet is a small raise into a growing pot, consistent with her aggressive history from the button (frequent light opens and 3-bets). Her range includes KK, QQ, AK suited, JJ, and bluffs like suited connectors or Ax suited. Shoving all-in applies maximum pressure, denying equity to her calling range while building the largest possible pot when ahead. Effective stack ~26BB; postflop play risks too much variance against deep Minni. Pot ~3400, calling 900 commits 5% more for implied odds, but shoving has better EV by folding out worse (e.g., AK ~30% equity) and isolating value. History shows Minni folds to heavy pressure often (e.g., Hand 120, 84).

I 4‑bet to 2100 with AcQc to isolate the short SB. Elon jammed/re‑raised to 3000 and is effectively very short (~815 behind). With position, decent blockers to his best combos (an Ace & Queen of clubs) and reasonable equity vs a polarized short‑stack range, I prefer to call the extra 900 (total 3000) rather than inflate the pot further. A min‑raise to 3815 would only extract his remaining chips now but also commits significantly more vs a range that likely contains many hands that dominate me (AA/KK/AK). Calling keeps the pot manageable, preserves post‑flop leverage (I can c‑bet favorable A/Q boards, control the pot on coordinated boards, and fold if the runout is clearly hostile), and still realizes solid equity against his shove/call range.

Flop Kd5d4c is draw-heavy with diamond flush draw, open-enders (A3, 63, etc.), but my AA remains an overpair and crushes her calling range of QQ-JJ, AK, AQ which often called the 5-bet pre. KK is possible but less likely given pre-flop sizing and her button aggression (often 4-bets light but calls heavy). With only 815 left (~13% pot) into 6100, I'm pot-committed; checking risks giving free cards to draws or allowing bluffs/check-raises, while shoving maximizes value from worse hands (e.g., QQ has ~20% equity), denies equity to all draws (~25-35% equity), and leverages fold equity against her history of folding to pressure (e.g., Hands 120, 84, 37). No fold equity concern as stacks deep behind, but optimal EV play is to bomb it.

Elon was very short and 3-bet/shoved preflop then put his last ~815 on the K♦5♦4♣ flop. The pot is 6,915 and I have to call 815 — about 10.5% equity required (815 / (6915+815)). With A♣Q♣ I block a lot of premium A/K combos, have backdoor club possibilities, and carry meaningful equity vs a polarized short-stack shove range (AA/KK/QQ/AK plus some Kx and diamond draws). The call is tiny relative to my stack and +EV given the pot odds, so I should see it through to realize equity. I call the 815 (total committed 3,815).

Preflop on the button in 3-handed play with 2h Ts offsuit, a very weak hand with poor connectivity and dominated by most calling ranges. Position is good for stealing blinds, but pot odds to call 100 into 150 are not compelling enough for a limp with trash, and raising risks being called or 3-bet by strong ranges from blinds who have shown aggression in history (e.g., frequent raises from Minni and Flash). Late in tournament (hand 142/150), better to preserve stack (7730) and wait for stronger hands to maximize EV and ladder up. Folding is disciplined and optimal.

With Elon folding on the button, I am heads-up against Minni in the big blind. Ace-Six offsuit is a strong enough hand to open for a raise in a 3-handed game from the small blind. I'll put in a standard 3x raise to 300 to take control of the pot and potentially win the blinds immediately.

I have 4♠9♣ — a weak, unsuited hand. Flash opened to 300 and I only need to put in 200 more. The pot is 400, so calling 200 (300 total commitment) gives ~3:1 pot odds (need ~25% equity). Against a typical SB opening range this hand has enough equity to defend, and I don’t want to turn 4-9o into a preflop 3-bet bluff or fold away a fair price in a heads-up spot. With deep stacks I can see a flop and make a disciplined decision: fold to a large continuation bet on dry boards, continue with any pair, two pair, or strong draws, and extract value when I improve. Calling keeps the pot manageable and preserves my stack for better spots.

I was the pre-flop aggressor and the flop of K-7-3 rainbow is very dry. This is a classic spot for a continuation bet. While I only have Ace-high, I represent a strong range that includes Kings, and Minni will find it difficult to continue without at least a pair. I will bet 300 (half pot) to try and take the pot down immediately.

I have 4♠9♣ on a dry 3h Kc 7s flop and Flash (SB) has made a small c-bet of 300 into a 900 pot. Calling 300 (pot ≈1200) gives ~25% break-even equity; my hand has ~24% to pair by the river (6 outs) plus remote backdoor straights. The bet is small relative to my 20,780 stack and SB’s opening range is likely wide heads‑up, so it’s worth seeing the turn cheaply. I won’t inflate the pot with a thin raise or bluff here — I’ll call, see the turn, fold to heavy aggression if I don’t improve, and extract value or apply pressure if I hit a pair or a strong draw.

On the turn, the 8s is relatively neutral but does bring a potential spade draw and completes a few unlikely straights (6-5). Having had my flop continuation bet called by Minni, it's likely she has a piece of the board, such as a 7, a small pocket pair, or a King. Checking here allows me to control the pot size and see how she reacts. If she checks behind, I can potentially win at showdown with Ace-high or see a cheap river. If she bets, I will have to evaluate the strength of my Ace-high against her betting range, but leading out again into a sticky player who has already called two streets is risky.

Board is 3♥ K♣ 7♠ 8♠ and I hold 4♠9♣. I only have 6 outs (three 4s + three 9s) to pair on the river — roughly ~13% equity — and with only one spade in hand (and two on board) a flush is not possible. Flash checked the turn after betting the flop; his range can be weak air turning into a bluff-catch or a strong hand slowing down, so a bet here is a thin, low-EV bluff that will get called by worse but fold out the relevant hands only some of the time. I committed cheaply to see the turn and planned to avoid inflating the pot with trash; I’ll stick to that plan: check and fold to significant river aggression unless I pair up or the river creates a very clear, credible bluffing opportunity. Conserves chips and leaves room to pick up a better spot later.

On the river, the board is 3h-Kc-7s-8s-2s, meaning three spades are out. I hold the Ace of Hearts and the Six of Spades, giving me Ace-high with the 6-high spade. I was the pre-flop aggressor and bet the flop, but Minni called. Both of us checked the turn. Minni's turn check suggests she might not have a King, or she's playing a marginal hand like a 7 or an 8 cautiously. Ace-high has some showdown value in a 3-handed game, but it's not strong enough to bet for value. By checking, I hope to reach a cheap showdown. If Minni bets, I will have to evaluate based on her sizing, but I am generally looking to fold to any significant aggression since my hand is just Ace-high.

I will check. With 4♠9♣ on 3♥ K♣ 7♠ 8♠ 2♠ I missed — no pair and not a flush (I have only four spades including my 4♠). Flash’s line (preflop raise, a small c-bet on the flop, then checking turn and river) looks like either a value Kx/7x/medium pair or a flop c-bet that he pot‑controls when checked to. The river check does not credibly fold enough of his calling/value range to make a bluff profitable here. The pot is only 1,200 and my hand has essentially no showdown value, so betting is thin-EDGE, high variance and -EV. I’ll check and concede to a river bet (fold) — preserve chips and wait for a better spot to apply pressure.

I am on the button with [Jd, 2h], which is a very weak hand. Given that we are nearing the end of the tournament (hand 143 of 150), preserving my stack for better starting hands is the priority. There is no reason to open-raise or call with such low equity.

Heads-up from the small blind with 3♥T♦ is a marginal hand, but in heads-up play you should open a wide range to seize initiative. Calling 50 leaves me out of position post‑flop with a weak holding; folding is too passive with deep stacks and only one caller left. I’ll open-raise to 3x the BB (300 total) to apply pressure and pick up the pot uncontested a lot of the time. The size gives decent fold equity versus Elon’s defending range but isn’t so large that I’m overcommitted with T3o — plan is to fold to a sizeable 3-bet, and if called play cautiously OOP (c-bet selective, give up to strong resistance). Stack sizes allow this pressure play without risking tournament life.

9d6s is a trash hand offsuit with no connectivity or high cards. Facing a standard SB raise to 300 after BTN folds, my BB defense range doesn't include this. Pot odds of calling 200 into 400 (2:1) are decent, but equity vs Minni's aggressive SB opening range (wide but still favors her) is poor (~30-35%), and postflop playability is terrible with reverse position. Minni is aggressive per history, but no need to speculate; folding preserves stack (76BB deep) for better spots near end of 150-hand tournament.

I’m on the button with T♣8♠ (off‑suit) in a three‑handed, deep‑stacked spot. In 3‑handed play my opening range should be wide and I want the initiative and positional advantage — limp/calling surrenders that. A standard 3x open to 300 is a clear, table‑standard size here: big enough to gain fold equity vs the SB/BB and isolate heads‑up, but small relative to my 20580 stack if I face a big 3‑bet. If I meet a large 3‑bet I’ll re‑evaluate and often fold this holding; if I get called I’ll play aggressively in position. Raising to 300 maximizes pressure while keeping risk controlled.

Qd7c is a marginal offsuit hand with poor playability postflop, especially out of position in the small blind against a button raise. Minni's button range is wide, but calling OOP risks difficult spots with reverse implied odds and poor equity realization. Late in the tournament (hand 144/150), folding preserves my stack (7580) for better spots without committing 250 chips (3.3% of stack) into a pot where I have ~35-40% equity at best against her range. No value in raising or calling.

I am in the Big Blind with 8d 3s, which is a very weak hand. The chip leader (Minni) raised from the button. Although the price to call is relatively small (200 more into a 450 pot), 83o performs poorly out of position and has almost no post-flop value or connectivity. With only 7 hands remaining in the tournament, I should prioritize preserving my stack rather than defending with marginal garbage hands against a large stack. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Pocket sixes is a premium hand in 3-handed play, especially on the button with position on both blinds. It has great set-mining equity, blocks overpairs somewhat, and plays well post-flop. With stacks around 75+ BB, raising standard 3x the big blind to isolate or steal blinds maximizes value. No prior action, so open-raise aggressively but not oversized. Opponents have shown aggression historically, but position favors me.

I hold pocket eights in the small blind, which is a very strong hand in 3-handed play. Elon has raised from the button, a position from which he likely has a wide range. Calling would put me out of position and allow the big blind, Minni, to enter the pot cheaply. I will 3-bet to 1000 to take initiative, define Elon's hand, and ideally isolate him or take the pot down immediately.

I’m in the big blind with 5♣4♠ facing Button open to 300 and an immediate 3‑bet to 1000 from the small blind. Pot is 1400 and it’s 900 to call (min‑raise to 1700). 5‑4 offsuit is a very weak, non‑blocking hand, and playing it out-of-position multiway against an opener + a 3‑bettor gives me very little equity. Calling 900 into 1400 (~1.56:1) requires ~39% equity which this hand won’t have versus these ranges, and raising would be committing chips with no fold equity or showdown value. Stack is healthy so there’s no reason to gamble here — fold and wait for a better spot.

Pocket sixes remains a premium hand heads-up with position on the SB after BB folds. Flash's 3bet from SB after a button open is likely polarized: strong hands like QQ+, AK, or bluffs/light 3bets given 3-handed dynamic. Folding is far too tight 3-handed (~20BB to call into 14BB pot, just 5:1 implied odds needed which we crush). 4betting risks cooler vs AA/KK/QQ and commits us deep-stacked; better to call and realize equity multiway postflop. We have excellent playability IP: set-mining potential, live cards for straights, dominates many Ax/broadways. Pot control allows check-fold weak flops, check-raise strong ones. Historical aggression from Flash suggests wide range we equity-realize against profitably. Call to induce bluffs/continue profitably.

On a flop of Kc-Qc-5s, there are two overcards to my pocket eights. As the pre-flop 3-better, this board texture actually favors my perceived range (which includes AK, AA, KK, QQ), but my actual hand (88) has significantly diminished in value. Elon calling my 3-bet suggests he has a range that frequently includes Kings, Queens, or pocket pairs. Betting here likely only gets called by hands that beat me and folds out hands I'm already ahead of (like smaller pairs or AJ). By checking, I can control the pot size and see how Elon reacts. If he checks back, I may have the best hand or can see a safe turn. If he bets, I will have to evaluate the sizing, but I am leaning towards a check-fold if he show strength, given the two overcards.

Heads-up on a K-Q-5 rainbow flop after Flash's SB 3-bet preflop and check. My pocket 6s is an underpair with good blocker to sets and strong playability, ahead of all bluffs and Ax in villain's polarized range (QQ+/AKo/s + light 3-bets like broadways/suited connectors). The board is fairly dry with minimal draws (possible weak gutshots like JT), so villain's check is often a miss (overcards or whiffs) or trap with top pair. Betting ~50-60% pot applies pressure, gets value from weaker Ax/Kx/Qx that flop middle pair or better but call light IP denies equity, and denies free cards to overcards that can improve. Checking risks giving free cards to live Ax or OESD turns, and pot control is less necessary IP with a hand this strong against the range. Deep stacks allow fold equity vs air. Historical data shows Flash aggressive but capable of checking weak flops; thin value bet maximizes EV near tournament end.

On this flop of Kc-Qc-5s, my pocket eights are now an underpair to two overcards. I checked to control the pot, and Elon responded with a significant bet of 1200 into a 2100 pot. Since Elon called a 3-bet pre-flop, his range is condensed toward medium pairs and high broadway cards, many of which (like KQ, KJ, QJ, or even AK/AQ) have me crushed on this board. Given that I am out of position and have very little equity to improve, calling here is a negative expected value play. I will fold to preserve my stack for the remaining hands of the tournament.

On the button in a 3-handed game, 3h 5h is a marginal opening hand. Given that we are at hand 146 of 150 and both Minni and Elon have shown significant aggression in recent hands (particularly hand #141 and #145), the risk of being 3-bet or forced into a difficult post-flop situation with a weak holding outweighs the small potential gain of stealing the blinds. To preserve my stack and maintain my current position in the tournament, I will choose the disciplined fold and wait for a higher equity opportunity in the remaining four hands.

Heads-up from the small blind with Q♥T♥ — a strong, playable hand heads-up. I’m out of position postflop, so I want to seize the initiative preflop and isolate the BB. Standard sizing here is about 3x the big blind; raising to 300 pressures weaker hands and gets value from calls while keeping postflop playability. If Elon 3-bets I’ll reassess based on size and pot odds; if he calls I have good equity and can continue. Total commitment shown is 300 (includes my posted small blind).

In the big blind facing a standard 3x raise to 300 from the small blind (Minni), with As2c offsuit. This is a weak ace with a terrible kicker and poor postflop playability. Minni's range is wide in SB vs BB, including many better aces, broadway cards, pairs, and suited hands that dominate A2o. Pot odds are favorable (200 to call into 400), but with deep stacks (86BB effective), implied odds are risky as I'll often be outkicked or drawing thin. Position postflop helps, but folding marginal hands preserves stack for better spots late in the tournament (hand 146/150) against aggressive opponents. No fold equity gained by calling thin; raising is off-table with this hand. Standard fold to avoid tough postflop decisions.

I’m on the button with 9♦5♦ facing a small open (bet brought to 200). 5-9 suited is speculative but playable in position: backdoor flush/straight potential and decent postflop playability. The raise is small relative to stacks and the BB still has to act, so I don’t want to inflate the pot with a marginal 3‑bet — better to see a flop in position. I’ll call to keep the pot controllable and realize equity; continue on flops that give me a diamond, a pair, or good straight draws, and fold to large aggression on dry boards.

J3o is a very weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and no high card strength. Facing a button limp, which could be a wide range, completing from the SB OOP risks playing postflop out of position with a hand that plays poorly multiway or heads-up against likely calling ranges. Pot odds are decent (50 to call into 250, ~4:1), but implied odds are low given the hand's lack of showdown value or drawing potential. BB still to act and may squeeze or check/call. With deep stacks and only 4 hands left after this, preserve stack for premium spots rather than spewing chips with trash. Standard fold.

I am in the Big Blind with 9s 7d. The Button (Minni) has limped in and the Small Blind (Elon) has folded. Since I have already posted the blind and there is no further cost to see the flop, I will check to see a free board with a mediocre hand. There is no strategic advantage to raising here with a hand like 9-7 offsuit against a player who has shown they can be aggressive or trap, especially as we are nearing the end of the tournament.

I have bottom pair (sevens) on a board with two overcards (Queen and Ten). Since the pot was limped pre-flop and I am out of position against the chip leader, the most prudent strategy is to check and see how the hand develops. Betting here is unlikely to fold out better hands and would only build a pot where I am likely behind or facing a variety of draws. Checking allows me to keep the pot small and see a turn card for free.

Flop is Q♥ T♣ 7♠ — I have 9♦5♦ (missed, no diamonds on board). Heads-up in position with a small pot (250) and the big blind checked. Flash’s checking range will include lots of weak broadways and medium pairs that will fold to pressure often. My hand has little showdown value, so checking behind forfeits fold equity and gives the opponent a free turn to realize equity. I’ll make a small c-bet to take the initiative and steal the pot: bet ~1/2 pot (125). Plan: if he folds I take the pot; if he calls I proceed cautiously on the turn and only continue if I improve or pick up clear additional equity; if he raises I’ll fold this hand.

I have bottom pair (sevens) with a nine kicker on a board of Qh-Tc-7s. The pot was limped pre-flop, and Minni has led out with a half-pot bet of 125. While I am behind any Queen or Ten, I have a made hand that is worth a call in a small pot, especially since Minni could be betting with a wide range of hands or even just air after I checked. With only a few hands left in the tournament, I want to avoid unnecessary risks, but folding a pair to a single small bet is too conservative. I will call to see the turn and re-evaluate based on the next card and her action.

The Ace of Spades on the turn is a significant scare card. There are now three overcards to my pair of sevens (Ace, Queen, and Ten), and the board is quite coordinated, making it likely that Minni has at least one of these or a draw that has now improved. As the Big Blind out of position against the chip leader, I have no incentive to bet into this board with bottom pair and a weak kicker. Checking is the most prudent play to see how Minni reacts and to try to get to a cheap showdown or fold if she shows strength.

I bet the flop and was called; the turn A is a scary card for my missed 9♦5♦ but Flash checked, showing some weakness (likely Qx/Tx/7x or a pot-control line). With a dry board and no diamond on the turn, I have almost no showdown value, so checking hands over gives him a free river. I’ll apply pressure with a half-pot turn bet (250) to represent an Ace or a strong Q and attempt to fold out single-pair hands that called the flop. Bet sizing balances fold equity while limiting loss if I’m called. If he raises I’ll fold; if he calls I’ll reassess on the river and look for a spot to bluff or give up depending on the card.

On a board with three overcards (Ace, Queen, and Ten), my bottom pair of sevens is extremely vulnerable. Minni has continued her aggression on the turn after the Ace appeared, which is a very strong card for her range. Given that the pot was limped and I am out of position, it is unlikely that my pair of sevens is the best hand or will improve to one. With the tournament nearing its conclusion, I will prioritize stack preservation and fold to her turn bet rather than calling and potentially facing another bet on the river.

Preflop on the button with 76o, a speculative offsuit connector with some straight potential. Position is excellent against the blinds. Pot is 150, to call 100. With deep stacks and near the end of the tournament (hand 148/150), stealing blinds is profitable as opponents have shown aggression but button range is wide. Raising to 300 (3x BB) is standard to take initiative, build pot with position, and apply pressure. Folding is too tight; calling risks multiway pot OOP for blinds. Raise exploits position and hand playability.

With only three hands remaining in the tournament and holding 9s 4c in the Small Blind, this is a clear fold. 94o is one of the weakest starting hands and playing it out of position against a Button raise from Elon is unprofitable. Given the tournament context and my current stack, there is no reason to risk chips with a garbage hand when I can wait for the final two hands to potentially find a better spot.

Fold. 2♣5♥ is one of the weakest hands and is poorly playable out of position facing an open from the button. Elon opened to 300; calling would cost 200 to win a 650 pot (need ~30.8% equity). 2-5 offsuit is usually dominated and offers little postflop playability — you’ll be out of position to frequent c-bets and face tough multi-street decisions. I have a large stack and no reason to spew chips in a marginal spot near the end of the match. If Elon were extremely loose I’d mix calls occasionally, but the +EV play here is to fold and wait for a better opportunity.

On the Button in a 3-handed game, Qc 9c is a strong hand that should almost always be played aggressively. With 150 hands total and this being hand 149, I am currently in second place. Raising allows me to take control of the pot and leverage my position against the blinds. A standard 3x raise to 300 is appropriate to pressure the shorter stack (Elon) and the chip leader (Minni).

I have pocket deuces (2d2h) in the small blind facing a button open to 300. I’ve already posted the 50 SB, so calling requires +250 (300 total). With deep stacks (my ~20.8k vs Flash 10.1k / Elon 8.6k) this is a textbook set-mining spot: calling keeps the pot controlled, preserves huge implied odds if I hit a set, and avoids turning a marginal hand into a bloated 3-bet showdown hand. I’m out of position to the button, which slightly reduces postflop playability, but that doesn’t outweigh the value of seeing a flop cheaply with a pocket pair. I’ll call to 300 total and re-evaluate on the flop (if the big blind squeezes I’ll fold or proceed cautiously depending on sizing).

Hand 149/150, BB with K9o facing button raise to 3BB and SB call. Pot 700, calling 200 more (total 300) for ~3.5:1 odds, requiring ~22% equity. K9o has good playability multiway, strong king blocker, and decent equity vs wide button opening range (Flash often raises button) and SB calling range (Minni flats frequently). Postflop position as BB allows control. Raising risks multiway pot with marginal hand; folding too tight late. Call to realize equity and see flop.

I called preflop as a set-mining call. The K♣7♠7♦ flop completely misses my 2♣2♦ (two overcards and a paired board), and I’m out of position to both opponents. Best line is to control the pot and try to realize my equity cheaply, so I’ll check and look for a free turn card. If I face meaningful pressure (a standard continuation bet or larger), I’ll fold — I’ll only entertain calling very small probes (roughly ≤1/3 pot ≈ 300) because the chance to hit my set by the river is only ~8.4% and I need a cheap price; if I do hit a deuce I’ll extract value aggressively.

Flop board Kc7s7d is paired and dry, favoring my top pair top kicker-ish (K9) multiway after calling preflop raise. Minni (SB) checking first suggests weakness, unlikely to have strong 77 or KK given flat pre. Flash (button raiser) has wide range including floats, worse kings (KQ,KJ,KT), 87s, overpairs that missed. Betting protects against gutshots (89,56) and gets value from weaker pairs/draws. Pot 900, bet 2/3 pot (600) balances value/protection without overbetting vulnerable hand late in tournament (hand 149/150). Stacks allow (83BB effective), position postflop decent. Checking risks free card to villainous button or multiway turn bloat.

On a board of Kc 7s 7d, my Qc 9c has very little equity. Elon leading out for 600 into two players (a 'donk bet') often represents a 7, a King, or a strong draw. With Minni (the chip leader) still to act behind me, calling is too risky and raising into a paired board against a lead is ill-advised given my hand strength. As this is hand 149 of 150, preserving my stack to secure my current position is the most logical move.

I checked the K♣‑7♠‑7♦ flop intending to see a free turn and fold to meaningful pressure. Elon’s bet to 600 (~40% of the 1500 pot) requires ~22% equity to justify a call, but my only realistic way to improve is hitting a set (~8.4% by the river). Most of Elon’s continuing range after calling preflop is weighted toward Kx, 7x or higher pairs that dominate my pocket deuces, so I’m not getting correct odds or fold equity. With healthy stack distance (I have 20,605 vs his 7,780) and this being a marginal set‑mining spot, folding preserves my stack for better spots rather than throwing chips away into a likely dominated situation.

On the button in a 3-handed spot J♥T♠ (off-suit) is a clear open-raise candidate — good connectivity and position make it profitable to take the initiative. With deep stacks (~200+ BB) and only the blinds to beat, I want a standard 3x open to build the pot, isolate the blinds, and capitalize on postflop position. Raising to 300 is consistent with table tendencies and is large enough to fold out weak blinds but small enough to keep weaker holdings and implied-odds hands in. Plan: if called, play aggressively in position with c-bets on favorable textures; if faced with a large 3-bet, fold to preserve my deep stack (or only continue vs a small, reasonable 3-bet where implied odds and reads justify it).

Q2s is a marginal suited hand at best, but from the small blind facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB), it's outside my defending range. Button ranges are wide, but Q2s plays poorly postflop OOP: dominated by better queens, weak kicker, limited straight potential, and flush draw requires specific boards. Calling commits 250 into a growing pot without great implied odds or fold equity. 3-betting is suboptimal as it's not strong enough for value or bluff. Folding preserves my 9230 stack (92BB deep) for the final hand without unnecessary risk, aligning with maximizing stack long-term despite being hand 150. Minni's aggressive history noted, but not enough to defend this trash.

This is the final hand of the tournament. I am in the big blind with a very weak hand (2s 3d). Minni has raised to 300 from the button, and Elon has folded. The pot is currently 450, and it costs me 200 more to call. Given that my goal is to maximize my stack and this is the last hand, there is no reason to risk chips with a bottom-tier hand out of position, even if the pot odds are somewhat attractive. Folding preserves my current stack of 10015.
